Bus_Cap Component: New York State Use of Preferred Name and Pronoun Legislation
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: This bill will require utilities and telephone service providers to allow customers to use their preferred name and/or preferred pronouns in all written or oral communications between the telephone service provider and the customer, as well as all statements or documentation relating to a customer's telephone service, regardless of whether such customer's preferred name differs from their current legal name. If a customer's preferred name differs from their current legal name, a telephone service provider may require such customer to provide reasonable proof of identity using their legal name, provided that such information shall be used solely to verify the customer's identity or for other purposes required by law.

Enhancements to credit rules to address multiple products on BSSe     Existing AIA customers with outstanding debt (past due balance) who are placing an order for a new product (i.e. wireless), via Salesforce, Opus or online will be prompted to pay outstanding debt in the new product order flow.      For sales orders that trigger Analyst Review due to outstanding debt (past due amount), the Credit & Activations agent may adjust the outstanding debt amount and allow the sales order to continue after a credit "refresh" is performed.

Device: Fiber w/ Wireless Backup (converged kit) subscriber: CGW450_x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Questions: _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Need to understand how returns will be supported for non-working CGW, due to 5G signal issue, or defective device as part of a bundle (EWCS). Do we need to return both devices?  Will this be handled as part of an M&P process?_x000D_ 2. What work in Oracle/Supply Chain should we consider from a solution/Development for returns_x000D_ 3. From a Converged Kit/Bundle – do we need a single SKU for CGW and EWCS, or Oracle Bill of materials SKU?_x000D_ 4. How do we support CGW and EWCS FW updates (need discussion with the WBB for Fiber as how provisioning and how it is going to be implemented? _x000D_ 5. Need clarity on supporting fallouts. Need fallout management support from each area of the business_x000D_ 6. How do we handle FTI for Converged Kit (CGW and EWCS orders)? _x000D_ _x000D_ Product Answers below: _x000D_ Converged Kit is dependent on the full build out of WBB (Wireless Broadband) functionality. It is assumed that the development of AIA can be leveraged for WBB. However, there are areas to where we would need to build WBB to support usage and qualification process. _x000D_ Wireless Backup (CGW device) service will ONLY be available as part of the Converged Kit and will not be available as a stand-alone/bolt-on service. Channel availability is only consumer.  _x000D_ • We will sell Fiber on BSSe with different flavor offers  _x000D_ • Standard fiber customer will get the BGW320_x000D_ • Premium fiber is the Converged Kit (includes ISA, and WBB (Wireless Back Up) CGW450, with EWCS). It is a bundled experience current pricing $25. Customer without the EWCS will continue to be billed the $25. _x000D_ • There will be instances that customer with the Premium offer (Converged Kit) may or may NOT require an extender (EWCS) depending on the customer’s situation. There should be a process to determine how many boosters are required.  NOTE: A disclosure will be required to support the grouping for these items. Marketing to confirm how to market without EWCS if found customer doesn’t need it.  _x000D_ • EWCS and CGW will be handled in Oracle with its own serial# and SKU. They need to be configurable. _x000D_ • For EWCS, if we are shipping out 3 or 4 devices. The expectation from the Supply chain side is that it will have 3- or 4-line items. _x000D_ • WBB/CGW will be pulled out of Supply Chain that includes the NSM ranges that were set up with Mobility.  This will be part of the pre-provisioning process.  This will be carried throughout the systems. Set up of the inventory will have the serial number, attributes including the ranges._x000D_ • SKU processing will require Premium Fiber (CGW450) SKU and for each EWCS (SKUs). ISA (Internet Security Advanced) added to the offer and will follow the same provisioning process in place today. _x000D_ • We will use the same SKU for CGW450 that is currently use for AIA vs Fiber from a SKU perspective will be the same. _x000D_ • NOTE: There are plans in the future that they are going to introduce a new CGW with a newer technology that maybe used for Converged kit in the future. For now, we will use the same SKU that AIA is using today for the Premium Fiber (Converged Kit) _x000D_ Return process: _x000D_ • Customer must return all devices when canceling Converged Kit_x000D_ • If it’s a Tech install, there will be some expectation – TBD on process_x000D_ • NOTE: Per Jacqui for CI changing equipment based on services ordered for added or removed is not supported on an existing customer for Converged Kit. For CI, if they cancel the Converged Kit, they will keep the CGW, and if they don’t retain the EWCS separate, then they can return it. _x000D_ • For future BSSe effort, we need to add return, and change equipment as part of the Fiber MVP. With the restriction, if you don’t order WBB with Converged kit, that means you can’t add later. Customer will not be downgraded to BGW. They will have to keep their CGW.  _x000D_ Qualification Process: _x000D_ 1. Not everyone will qualify for WBB. Customer signing up with the Premium offer must go to through a qualification similar to AIA. The qualification process will not be strict as what AIA’s qualification process for WBB on fiber. Qualification will have different criteria. WBB doesn’t have to have a great connection as per Amy Hollister. _x000D_ 2. As part of the WBB installation process, is to check the wireless back up signal. If the signal is low, part of the qualification process includes a prediction we assumed that the customer has strong & enough signal to where they can use the WBB. If we find something in the installation process that FTI and customer cannot find adequate signal, then the customer will be given two options _x000D_ • Cancel the premium, and have standard fiber  _x000D_ • Keep the Premium experience, bill the premium charge, but will not get the WBB (not offering the discount rate). _x000D_ NOTE: As part of the order flow process, there will be disclaimers around the WBB services regarding predicting adequate signal.  _x000D_ Example: There are instances in most of the new homes that the fiber jack is tucked in a closet or basement, which then causes wireless signal to be very poor. Hence, not get good signal.  Per Amy, it needs to be check as part of the install process. For CSI, there will be a way in the process that checks the wireless signal. For FTI, they will need to follow the M&P, and or will require the signal strength to successfully do the install. _x000D_ Support for EWCS/Booster (Extended WiFi Coverage Service) from an FTI perspective_x000D_ • Whatever process we have today to install the booster from an FTI perspective will be followed per Jacqui and Bharat_x000D_ • Tech guy carries the device – will have this taken care of in supply chain. Per Leilani, she included in her scope – “The ability for the FT to use the equipment that is at the site to make sure it is working, if the transaction started out as a CSI”_x000D_ Provisioning of WBB _x000D_ • SIM card will be preinstalled in the device. Similar to how AIA is provisioned. It is part of the account order set up where the SIM is provisioned. The customer will go to the installation process, which will have checkpoint to make sure the device is getting adequate signal in order to activate the WBB. _x000D_ Are AIA and WBB both are fix - Yes_x000D_ • If WBB is the only service, the customer will be provided recommendation where to place the device in order to get the best signal. For new install, it will be part of the new fiber M&Ps to choose the best location to get better signal. Some of the places we install fiber today, cannot be beneficial for wireless signal._x000D_ Customer inventory would need to know when devices are shipped to the customer and should know what is in the customer premise. Per Jacqui, Supply Chain is supposed to do an immediate update to the customer’s account. _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_

Bus_Cap Component: Billing: Real Time Biller Suppress Invoice
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Billing (Carla Rubio): Real Time Biller_x000D_   - Real Time Biller will require a core change to add functionality to NOT continue to create an invoice when there is a $0 balance and $0 charges.   _x000D_   - There will NOT be a bill ready notification sent to the customer but they will see the invoice in the online view.   _x000D_   - A zero rated bill will essentially be created for perpetuity until the account becomes inactive, which is probably 6-8 months post cancellation as we have to refund the NRF fee if they return the equipment up to 6 months post cancellation.   _x000D_  _x000D_  Per Whitney Falconer, BDO, align to backlog/future work to be fleshed out and further prioritized by business.

Bus_Cap Component: C1: Display more than one promo
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Billing (Carla Rubio) - Promo Bill Displays_x000D_ Only one option to display promo in CI1.  Need to customize bill formats to display to mirror desired output to mirror promotions loaded in Catalog One._x000D_ Additional clarifications obtained from prod/business client:_x000D_ 1. MRC Promo Expiration Date Display -Actual expiration date (expires Mmm dd, YYYY) _x000D_ 2.Promo Counter – For promotions on MRCs (not promotions on OTCs), no credit counter is being requested. When OTC promotions are discussed, we’ll likely introduce a counter at that point.  _x000D_ 3. MRC Promo Non-Roll Up Display -Roll-up promos will display first , with sort. Non-roll up promos display next, with sort_x000D_ 4. Support ability to display or not display a credited charge. For example, if I have a $99 activation fee but I am giving a $99 activation fee credit, the business wants the ability to show the charge and credit or suppress both._x000D_ 5. OTC Installment / Promo - We need a counter for the One Time Charge which can be paid in installments over three bill cycles.   At times we may have a promo that waives 100% of the onetime charge or a % of the onetime charge. We will need to support a one line as well as a  two line display for OTC  PROMO counter on the bill invoice.  Whatever amount is charged to the customer can then be paid in installments. _x000D_ There is a separate line item in the IDR related to install fees and the ability to pay via installments.

Customers can add the following additional services to their broadband order at the point of sale.  Customers can also add these services any time after installation as a separate order. BSSe will determine and present eligible WLS, TV and Internet offers in Top Offers.  BSSe will determine and show products of interest based on a Consumer or Signature (IRU) customer's needs and history.  Existing customer upgrade eligibility rules apply to any offers displayed.  Agent will review the applicable offer details in Customer Connect to determine steps for adding the offer...    Top Offers is present in OPUS currently.  This EPIC would make it available in ACC.  Top Offers - OPUS  WLS:   Display the top offers the customer is eligible for to the agent in the buy flow and, in the case of Wireless,  once selected, automatically populate in the Cart.   Two places that Top Offers appear in the buy flow:   1. Dashboard – can click on them to save them and it sets a little blue flag  2. Top Offers Screen  For WLS, offers are automated – the system will give the customer what they are eligible for based on what is in their cart.  It does not matter if you use the Top Offers tool/screen (30% click by agent).  They can use to discuss offers with customer.    Agents use OPUS Sales Tool (MFT) to talk to customers and build the quote manually, add offers manually.     In TLG there are a few offers that cannot be automated – examples*:    1. BYOD Smartphone Offer because it is a discount/promo.    2. Cross Sell offers are also discounts so we cannot automate them in TLG.   All offers in BSSe should be automated and auto-populated in the CARE once selected.  Currently, National, non-personalized device offers are displayed via Top Offers.  Further discussion needed to determine if we should only show personalized Top Offers.   The buy flow should prompt rep that a customer has BB and not wireless, for example.  This should be taken into account when displaying a top offer.   Top Offers Broadband  Top Offers for BB is built into the buy flow as you are picking the product.   Promos are built into the product/speed you choose.  Once selected, the CART is automatically populated.  Cannot do this for mobility as there are multiple combinations of devices and speeds.    * Today, WLS TLG can apply credit against a charge or not against a charge – shows as a credit on the bill.  (This is not how BSSe works currently).  For a device promo, this works because it does not impact taxes (customer pays device tax at POS).    BYOD promo cannot be applied to nothing, it must go against the MRC and it impacts taxes. This discount is not built in the catalog but directly into TLG.    In BSSe, everything will be built in Catalog 1 and will be automated.

BSSe will determine and show products of interest based on a Consumer or Signature (IRU) customer's needs and history.  Existing customer upgrade eligibility rules apply to any offers displayed.  Agent will review the applicable offer details in Customer Connect to determine steps for adding the offer...    Top Offers is present in OPUS currently.  This EPIC would make it available in ACC.  Top Offers - OPUS  WLS:   Display the top offers the customer is eligible for to the agent in the buy flow and, in the case of Wireless,  once selected, automatically populate in the Cart.   Two places that Top Offers appear in the buy flow:   1. Dashboard – can click on them to save them and it sets a little blue flag  2. Top Offers Screen  For WLS, offers are automated – the system will give the customer what they are eligible for based on what is in their cart.  It does not matter if you use the Top Offers tool/screen (30% click by agent).  They can use to discuss offers with customer.    Agents use OPUS Sales Tool (MFT) to talk to customers and build the quote manually, add offers manually.     In TLG there are a few offers that cannot be automated – examples*:    1. BYOD Smartphone Offer because it is a discount/promo.    2. Cross Sell offers are also discounts so we cannot automate them in TLG.   All offers in BSSe should be automated and auto-populated in the CARE once selected.  Currently, National, non-personalized device offers are displayed via Top Offers.  Further discussion needed to determine if we should only show personalized Top Offers.   The buy flow should prompt rep that a customer has BB and not wireless, for example.  This should be taken into account when displaying a top offer.   Top Offers Broadband  Top Offers for BB is built into the buy flow as you are picking the product.   Promos are built into the product/speed you choose.  Once selected, the CART is automatically populated.  Cannot do this for mobility as there are multiple combinations of devices and speeds.    * Today, WLS TLG can apply credit against a charge or not against a charge – shows as a credit on the bill.  (This is not how BSSe works currently).  For a device promo, this works because it does not impact taxes (customer pays device tax at POS).    BYOD promo cannot be applied to nothing, it must go against the MRC and it impacts taxes. This discount is not built in the catalog but directly into TLG.    In BSSe, everything will be built in Catalog 1 and will be automated.

Provide a way to bill multiple products (AIA/WLS, Fiber/WLS) on one bill.  This will require ability to qualify multiple products,  charge any POS charge for multiple products, charge taxes for multiple products, apply multiple product discounts, perform credit/fraud checks for multiple product, provide bill estimates for all products, support the CART & Checkout for all products.

Provide capability for AT&T  (currently Jeff Dietrich's team), to develop queries against a customer database (ex: Snowflake) to obtain a list of accounts that require changes via Mass Updates.      Promos (ex: Cross Sell) offers across products and billers may not be able to be added during the buy flow as is the north star capability.  Scripts may need to be run to identify that customers have the products specified in the cross sell offer and the promo applied to subsequent bills.      Queries may need to be run to identify customers who were billed incorrectly  or experienced an outage, for example, in order to make corrections via Mass Updates.

App that allows you to know where your kids are, manage screen time, & limit what they access online.  Includes support for up to 10 family members & up to 30 devices.    SME - DiSonya Young (de789v)    Cost - $7.99/mo  Subscribers - 112K  Annual Revenue - $10M    Future roadmap will include-     iOS Parental Controls: iOS Parent can allow/deny child access to change device/phone passcode, make-in app purchases, and change the device date a time settings.    Driving features: check in on a teenager's road trip with friends, verify that a child's bus driver has safe driving habits, safe driving score, receive alert when a child has been involved in a potential collision.    Manage Contact: parents can block unwanted contacts and allow trusted contacts on the child's device by saving the contact phone number as blocked or trusted. Parent can also view the top contacts the child interacts with.    These features were submitted for privacy review recently so there is no definitive date to be in market, but roadmap projection is sometime in 2025.

Blocks mobile purchases like apps that are billed directly to your wireless account.      Customers can add and remove Purchase Blocker through the myAT&T App or at myAT&T online (att.com/mobilepurchases)    Purchase Blocker can also be added via OPUS SOC code - PB01    -Purchase Blocker may not be added to a line that is suspended.  -Purchase Blocker must be removed in order for a customer to make a charitable donation via text.  -Purchase Blocker is a free feature.  -Purchase Blocker does not restrict the ability to make mobile purchases with other forms of payment, such as a credit card purchases for things like apps from the Apple or Google stores).    3/1/2024: The California Public Utilities Commission requires an annual customer notification regarding the availability of third-party purchase blocking options, notifications for this year will begin on the March 2024 wireless bills.      SME - Lynne Mackinney (tm4547)

Customers can add and remove Purchase Blocker through the myAT&T App or at myAT&T online (att.com/mobilepurchases)    Purchase Blocker can also be added via OPUS SOC code - PB01    -Purchase Blocker may not be added to a line that is suspended.  -Purchase Blocker must be removed in order for a customer to make a charitable donation via text.  -Purchase Blocker is a free feature.  -Purchase Blocker does not restrict the ability to make mobile purchases with other forms of payment, such as a credit card purchases for things like apps from the Apple or Google stores).    3/1/2024: The California Public Utilities Commission requires an annual customer notification regarding the availability of third-party purchase blocking options, notifications for this year will begin on the March 2024 wireless bills. Cobrowse software, commonly referred to as collaborative browsing, allows for our agents and our customers to view and interact with the same page or mobile application interface simultaneously, in real-time. This enables seamless collaboration between our agents and customers making it easier and more efficient to troubleshoot issues, demonstrate features, or guide customers through completing a digital task.     Flexibility in its configurability  Supports native iOS & Android mobile apps as well as desktop and mobile browsers.   Configurable to work with built in house systems such as CRM and live chat.   Designated user role permissions.   Security & Privacy  Self-hosting or on prem based on business needs.   Data redaction to ensure customer privacy.   Data encryption both at rest and in transit using AES-256 or stronger and TLS 1.2+.  Performance  Minimal size SDK helps maintain optimal app performance.    Screen highlighting to call attention to any part of the screen for the customer.     Annotating the screen to guide the user through a support request or completing a task.     Remote control access temporarily allowing the agent to take specified actions on behalf of the customer.     3rd party content enables sharing of content such as bill PDFs or Customer Service Summary which are outside our apps.     Session recording which creates a video for every cobrowse session for auditing and training purposes.     Cross domain cobrowsing in the event customers need to navigate multiple sites or apps.

Bus_Cap Component: VD2 - International - Canada/Mexico ILD & Roaming & PPU
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Created new SRT labeled VD2 - International - Canada/Mexico ILD & Roaming & PPU after alignment meeting on 7/16 with Rubio, Tupy, & Anna    Anna confirmed work for this epic will start in PI22    For: International travelers who are AT&T customers  Who: Need seamless, affordable, and easily managed international calling and data services  The: AT&T International Services Epic  That: Provides unlimited wireless BSSe domestic rate plans, including unlimited calling from the U.S. to Mexico and Canada   (JP - Latin America is coming in a later phase)  Unlike: Competing international calling plans which are often complicated, expensive, and lack comprehensive coverage  Our Solution: Offers a user-friendly, cost-effective, and comprehensive set of international calling and data services with transparent and straightforward options for travelers.    Needs to include:  Free Canada/Mexico ILD & roaming, usage, ILD add-on/PPU & PPU. Must include Voice Usage Communications (ex. Notification if your PPU exceeds $xx)    SMEs:  Leslie McGill (lm4984), Bobbie Tracy (bt1468)

Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Installation Type: Customers who require full tech installation (first time fiber installation at home) will not be eligible to waive FTI without assistance of promotion or manager override (Configurable by business)_x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers(BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Should this dispatch on demand fee be in addition to one time tech install fee? – The business would like the Dispatch on Demand fee to be a configurable fee that can be added to customers that are eligible for CSI but refuse and request a FTI.  The dispatch on demand will be charged in place of the FTI fee.  There should not be a situation where both fees are required._x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(BB 4):_x000D_ (LC) Allowing technicians to add service / equipment to orders is part of fulfillment process and should be integrated with RTI for shopping and order submission at or after the time of install.  Added to Fulfillment support for FTI epic.

Installation Type:  Customers at an address which has previously had Fiber will qualify for Customer Self Install (CSI)._x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Question(s): _x000D_ Will this perform like Legacy today?_x000D_ Plans/impacts of CPE?_x000D_ Will we require an FTI if the Fiber Jack is of a certain age?_x000D_ Are Fiber Jacks agnostice to GPON and XGS PON?_x000D_ Can customers decline CSI in favor of a fee based FTI?_x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(s):_x000D_ Revised Detailed Desctiption:_x000D_ Installation Type:  Customers at an address which has a Fiber Jack and with Fiber present all the way to the Remote Gateway will qualify for Customer Self Install (CSI)._x000D_ Initial Feedback:_x000D_ Key for CSI is that a property has a fiber jack installed.  The assumption is that any customer that has fiber installed and utilized a BGW320 would have a fiber jack installed.  The supply chain issues pushed more ONO/ONT and BGW210 that in my opinion would not qualify for CSI, pushing o FTI to install the fiber into the home with a fiber jack._x000D_ 7/12 Meeting Notes:_x000D_ -Cannot have any Copper in play for a CSI, need to have a Fiber Jack and Fiber all the way to the RB_x000D_ -If a customer or the previous customer has/had a BGW010 or BGW210 we would FTI the installation._x000D_ -Fiber Jacks are agnostic to GPON and XGS PON, completely passive._x000D_ -Older fiber jacks (fiber in/copper out) have been removed from the system._x000D_ -Capability for customer ability to decline CSI has not yet been submitted by business as a capability._x000D_ _x000D_ Note:  Per PDO - This capability is already covered by an enabler EPIC 39412, which did not have product input. Team agrees Danyelle's epic take precedence
Installation Type:  Multi Gig, 2G and 5G speeds will be CSI eligible if the location has an XGS splitter and a fiber jack installed

Network:  Real-time facility assignment_x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Question(s): combined feedback for List # 109, 169._x000D_ What are we looking at for Real-time facility assighment?_x000D_ How is capacity planning going to be done?_x000D_ Any difference in how a CSI and FTI is handled?_x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response:_x000D_ Revised Detailed Desctiption:_x000D_ Installation Type:  Customers at an address which has a Fiber Jack and with Fiber present all the way to the Remote Gateway will qualify for Customer Self Install (CSI)._x000D_ Initial Feedback:_x000D_ Legacy stack has the "dual XGS" configuration due to the fact that XGS is a subtype of GPON.  We need to configure unique/distinct transport types, one for GPON to serve speed tiers below 1GB and XGSPON for speeds 1GB and above. Another point here is that we need to make sure that once a customer is provisioned on XGS, that customer stays on XGS for all speed tiers.  It also identifies the facilities to be used at time of order.  _x000D_ 7/12 Meeting Notes:_x000D_ There is a team looking at exhaust conditions for PFPs and a fallout process that creates a SHOTS ticket for C&E when capacity is exhausted._x000D_ -In BSSE we need to fix the subtype issue, created previously where XGS is a sub-type of GPON.  _x000D_ -Real-time facility assighment is needed to utilize the imbedded GPON capacity and therefore, 1gig and below speed tiers should be put on GPON.  Once the capacity is exhausted all assignments, regardless of speed tier should be made on XGS._x000D_ i.    Identify capacity exhaust scenarios in the ordering system_x000D_ ii.    Push out the provisioning date by configurable(~19) number of days_x000D_ iii.    Issue an automated SHOTS(7838) ticket to resolve the capacity exhaust_x000D_ iv.    Complete the order submission_x000D_ -This pertains to FTI only and assumes CSI will have facilities in place._x000D_ -Assume assigned OLT ports will support CSI.

Another point here is that we need to make sure that once a customer is provisioned on XGS, that customer stays on XGS for all speed tiers.  It also identifies the facilities to be used at time of order.  _x000D_ 7/12 Meeting Notes:_x000D_ There is a team looking at exhaust conditions for PFPs and a fallout process that creates a SHOTS ticket for C&E when capacity is exhausted._x000D_ -In BSSE we need to fix the subtype issue, created previously where XGS is a sub-type of GPON.  _x000D_ -Real-time facility assighment is needed to utilize the imbedded GPON capacity and therefore, 1gig and below speed tiers should be put on GPON.  Once the capacity is exhausted all assignments, regardless of speed tier should be made on XGS._x000D_ i.    Identify capacity exhaust scenarios in the ordering system_x000D_ ii.    Push out the provisioning date by configurable(~19) number of days_x000D_ iii.    Issue an automated SHOTS(7838) ticket to resolve the capacity exhaust_x000D_ iv.    Complete the order submission_x000D_ -This pertains to FTI only and assumes CSI will have facilities in place._x000D_ -Assume assigned OLT ports will support CSI.

Full Tech Installation (FTI) : Fiber only_x000D_ •  Technician can add on to account for customer based on their internet needs determined at point of installation_x000D_ •  Technician can add on WIFI extenders to account and install for customer.  Technician will determine the appropriate numbers of extenders based on WIFI Home mapping. _x000D_      -  Customer has ability to add more or reduce amount post installation through App and CARE without exceeding limit of WIFI extenders determined by AT&T. _x000D_ •  Technician can add on other features to the customer’s account at point of installation (ex. Advanced Security)_x000D_ •  Technicians will be compensated for any eligible add-ons. _x000D_ •  Technician will test signal to confirm completion of installation which will trigger the account to confirm completion of installation to begin billing. _x000D_ •  If the technician is unable to install the service due to the customer not being home at the time of installation, the technician can code installation appointment as a no show which will trigger the customer to reschedule the appointment for installation_x000D_   -  Customer will not be charged a fee if they are not available for installation at scheduled time._x000D_ •  If the technician cannot install Fiber at the designated location, the technician will enter in a no installation code.  Links to the current codes in Fiber PSD.   See Fiber PSD Appendix for code examples._x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers(BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Should this dispatch on demand fee be in addition to one time tech install fee? – The business would like the Dispatch on Demand fee to be a configurable fee that can be added to customers that are eligible for CSI but refuse and request a FTI.  The dispatch on demand will be charged in place of the FTI fee.  There should not be a situation where both fees are required._x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(BB 4):_x000D_ (LC) Allowing technicians to add service / equipment to orders is part of fulfillment process and should be integrated with RTI for shopping and order submission at or after the time of install.  Added to Fulfillment support for FTI epic.

Advanced Security)_x000D_ •  Technicians will be compensated for any eligible add-ons. _x000D_ •  Technician will test signal to confirm completion of installation which will trigger the account to confirm completion of installation to begin billing. _x000D_ •  If the technician is unable to install the service due to the customer not being home at the time of installation, the technician can code installation appointment as a no show which will trigger the customer to reschedule the appointment for installation_x000D_   -  Customer will not be charged a fee if they are not available for installation at scheduled time._x000D_ •  If the technician cannot install Fiber at the designated location, the technician will enter in a no installation code.  Links to the current codes in Fiber PSD.   See Fiber PSD Appendix for code examples._x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers(BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Should this dispatch on demand fee be in addition to one time tech install fee? – The business would like the Dispatch on Demand fee to be a configurable fee that can be added to customers that are eligible for CSI but refuse and request a FTI.  The dispatch on demand will be charged in place of the FTI fee.  There should not be a situation where both fees are required._x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(BB 4):_x000D_ (LC) Allowing technicians to add service / equipment to orders is part of fulfillment process and should be integrated with RTI for shopping and order submission at or after the time of install.  Added to Fulfillment support for FTI epic.

Bus_Cap Component: Converged Kit Ordering and Sign Up - DEFERRED
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: AMENDED SCOPE:   Effective 3Q2022, the CPE strategy for Fiber CI has been modified to no longer offer the CGW450 device and Wireless Back-up as an add-on in the Converged Kit.  The business will want Wireless Backup at Fiber MVP.   While the CGW450 will NOT be offered as we know it today, there will be a new TBD device name.    _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ Converged Kit Ordering/Sign Up:_x000D_  - Accept T&Cs and TOS and any required disclosures associated with bundle_x000D_  - When Converged Kit is added to a broadband account, conflicting add-on features such as boosters or extenders and the $10 CPE fee (if on account) will automatically be removed_x000D_  - Provide customer recommendations on volume of compatible Boosters to include with their package (as low as zero, with a cap likely around 3-5)_x000D_ - Ability for customer to add incremental Extenders beyond the Converged Kit limit for a low monthly bolt-on cost (incremental to Converged Kit cost, separate line item on bill)_x000D_  - Periodically provide contextual offer to existing customers to purchase Converged Kit (if not on account) through myAT&T and Smart Home Manager_x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture questions: _x000D_ 1. Need to understand how returns will be supported for non-working CGW, due to 5G signal issue, or defective device as part of a bundle (EWCS). Do we need to return both devices?  Will this be handled as part of an M&P process?_x000D_ 2. What work in Oracle/Supply Chain should we consider from a solution/Development for returns_x000D_ 3. From a Converged Kit/Bundle – do we need a single SKU for CGW and EWCS, or Oracle Bill of materials SKU?_x000D_ 4. How do we support CGW and EWCS FW updates (need discussion with the WBB for Fiber as how provisioning and how it is going to be implemented? _x000D_ 5. Need clarity on supporting fallouts. Need fallout management support from each area of the business_x000D_ 6. How do we handle FTI for Converged Kit (CGW and EWCS orders)? _x000D_ _x000D_ Product Answers below: _x000D_ Converged Kit is dependent on the full build out of WBB (Wireless Broadband) functionality. It is assumed that the development of AIA can be leveraged for WBB. However, there are areas to where we would need to build WBB to support usage and qualification process. _x000D_ Wireless Backup (CGW device) service will ONLY be available as part of the Converged Kit and will not be available as a stand-alone/bolt-on service. Channel availability is only consumer.  _x000D_ • We will sell Fiber on BSSe with different flavor offers  _x000D_ • Standard fiber customer will get the BGW320_x000D_ • Premium fiber is the Converged Kit (includes ISA, and WBB (Wireless Back Up) CGW450, with EWCS). It is a bundled experience current pricing $25. Customer without the EWCS will continue to be billed the $25. _x000D_ • There will be instances that customer with the Premium offer (Converged Kit) may or may NOT require an extender (EWCS) depending on the customer’s situation. There should be a process to determine how many boosters are required.  NOTE: A disclosure will be required to support the grouping for these items. Marketing to confirm how to market without EWCS if found customer doesn’t need it.  _x000D_ • EWCS and CGW will be handled in Oracle with its own serial# and SKU. They need to be configurable. _x000D_ • For EWCS, if we are shipping out 3 or 4 devices. The expectation from the Supply chain side is that it will have 3- or 4-line items. _x000D_ • WBB/CGW will be pulled out of Supply Chain that includes the NSM ranges that were set up with Mobility.  This will be part of the pre-provisioning process.  This will be carried throughout the systems. Set up of the inventory will have the serial number, attributes including the ranges._x000D_ • SKU processing will require Premium Fiber (CGW450) SKU and for each EWCS (SKUs). ISA (Internet Security Advanced) added to the offer and will follow the same provisioning process in place today. _x000D_ • We will use the same SKU for CGW450 that is currently use for AIA vs Fiber from a SKU perspective will be the same. _x000D_ • NOTE: There are plans in the future that they are going to introduce a new CGW with a newer technology that maybe used for Converged kit in the future. For now, we will use the same SKU that AIA is using today for the Premium Fiber (Converged Kit) _x000D_ Return process: _x000D_ • Customer must return all devices when canceling Converged Kit_x000D_ • If it’s a Tech install, there will be some expectation – TBD on process_x000D_ • NOTE: Per Jacqui for CI changing equipment based on services ordered for added or removed is not supported on an existing customer for Converged Kit. For CI, if they cancel the Converged Kit, they will keep the CGW, and if they don’t retain the EWCS separate, then they can return it. _x000D_ • For future BSSe effort, we need to add return, and change equipment as part of the Fiber MVP. With the restriction, if you don’t order WBB with Converged kit, that means you can’t add later. Customer will not be downgraded to BGW. They will have to keep their CGW.  _x000D_ Qualification Process: _x000D_ 1. Not everyone will qualify for WBB. Customer signing up with the Premium offer must go to through a qualification similar to AIA. The qualification process will not be strict as what AIA’s qualification process for WBB on fiber. Qualification will have different criteria. WBB doesn’t have to have a great connection as per Amy Hollister. _x000D_ 2. As part of the WBB installation process, is to check the wireless back up signal. If the signal is low, part of the qualification process includes a prediction we assumed that the customer has strong & enough signal to where they can use the WBB. If we find something in the installation process that FTI and customer cannot find adequate signal, then the customer will be given two options _x000D_ • Cancel the premium, and have standard fiber  _x000D_ • Keep the Premium experience, bill the premium charge, but will not get the WBB (not offering the discount rate). _x000D_ NOTE: As part of the order flow process, there will be disclaimers around the WBB services regarding predicting adequate signal.  _x000D_ Example: There are instances in most of the new homes that the fiber jack is tucked in a closet or basement, which then causes wireless signal to be very poor. Hence, not get good signal.  Per Amy, it needs to be check as part of the install process. For CSI, there will be a way in the process that checks the wireless signal. For FTI, they will need to follow the M&P, and or will require the signal strength to successfully do the install. _x000D_ Support for EWCS/Booster (Extended WiFi Coverage Service) from an FTI perspective_x000D_ • Whatever process we have today to install the booster from an FTI perspective will be followed per Jacqui and Bharat_x000D_ • Tech guy carries the device – will have this taken care of in supply chain. Per Leilani, she included in her scope – “The ability for the FT to use the equipment that is at the site to make sure it is working, if the transaction started out as a CSI”_x000D_ Provisioning of WBB _x000D_ • SIM card will be preinstalled in the device. Similar to how AIA is provisioned. It is part of the account order set up where the SIM is provisioned. The customer will go to the installation process, which will have checkpoint to make sure the device is getting adequate signal in order to activate the WBB. _x000D_ Are AIA and WBB both are fix - Yes_x000D_ • If WBB is the only service, the customer will be provided recommendation where to place the device in order to get the best signal. For new install, it will be part of the new fiber M&Ps to choose the best location to get better signal. Some of the places we install fiber today, cannot be beneficial for wireless signal._x000D_ Customer inventory would need to know when devices are shipped to the customer and should know what is in the customer premise. Per Jacqui, Supply Chain is supposed to do an immediate update to the customer’s account. _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_

Bus_Cap Component: Add-on:  Extenders & EWCS during FTI by Tech + Compensation
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Customers can also add these services any time after installation as a separate order:_x000D_  - Converged Kit All-Fi Pro_x000D_  - Boosters (extenders) All-Fi Plus_x000D_  - Advanced Security_x000D_ _x000D_ Technicians can assist the customer to amend the order to add services at point of install._x000D_ _x000D_ MODIFICATION MADE 5/24/22 Requiring SRB Approval (updated on 7/27/22 per input from Jacqui Peters):   _x000D_ _x000D_ Enhanced FTI Installation for EWCS (Extended Wifi Coverage Services):_x000D_ _x000D_ When a technician is in the field with a customer, the technician should be able to add EWCS with customer’s permission._x000D_ o Technicians should not be required to wait for order process to be able to continue extender installation._x000D_ o Technician must be required to capture customer approval as required (ex: collect T&C agreement, review pricing)_x000D_ o Technicians should be able to install as many extenders as needed within one session._x000D_ o System should allow auto-discovery of extenders. If auto-discovery fails or is delayed (value for delayed must be configurable by the business), system should allow the technician to scan the equipment being installed._x000D_ o Based on triggering of adding EWCS, all appropriate systems will be updated with the added service and equipment._x000D_ o Any scenarios where the technician has to await a response to continue in the flow must support a business defined SLA_x000D_ _x000D_ Other Guidance:_x000D_ _x000D_ - Want the process between both enabler and BSSe to be the same or better on BSSe_x000D_ - Legacy Vision: _x000D_ o Tech scans device_x000D_ o Notifies apps of adding the extender service to the account_x000D_ o Scan, Plug, Pair and go for technician…triggers activation, ordering (if needed), billing, global supply chain, nimbus_x000D_              -Display price screen isn’t necessarily ordering screen it’s notification to customer of pricing change, T&C, and documenting customers agreement_x000D_              - Nimbus allow for the customer to activate the extenders even before EWCS is ordered/assigned to the customer_x000D_ o Technician can add EWCS by either scanning the extender _x000D_              -Scan ties the extender Serial Number with the account number (For asset tracking and activation)_x000D_              - Need to define expected business response times_x000D_ - Scenario and BSSe vision: Tech arrives at job (Gateway only on the order)(big house need extenders)_x000D_ o Tell Atlas what doing (add extenders)( this triggers flow for T&C, pricing, T&C, signature (whatever is necessary for the add-on)_x000D_ o Tech plugs in extender and network auto-discovers extender_x000D_  On successful auto-discovery, the extenders will activate and tech continues with install_x000D_ o If auto-discovery doesn’t happen within a configurable period of time (ex. 30 sec), technician is able to scan the equipment and system will tie the extender to the account_x000D_ o Atlas flow adding EWCS should support adding multiple extenders within one flow_x000D_ - 4 scenarios_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on existing order_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on repair order_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on install and need to add extender_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on repair and needed a helper ticket_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ Note:  QB 2214 is making these FTI redesign changes to the current stack, and the Architecture Council has approved current stack work. Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Customers can also add these services any time after installation as a separate order:_x000D_  - Converged Kit All-Fi Pro_x000D_  - Boosters (extenders) All-Fi Plus_x000D_  - Advanced Security_x000D_ _x000D_ Technicians can assist the customer to amend the order to add services at point of install._x000D_ _x000D_ MODIFICATION MADE 5/24/22 Requiring SRB Approval (updated on 7/27/22 per input from Jacqui Peters):   _x000D_ _x000D_ Enhanced FTI Installation for EWCS (Extended Wifi Coverage Services):_x000D_ _x000D_ When a technician is in the field with a customer, the technician should be able to add EWCS with customer’s permission._x000D_ o Technicians should not be required to wait for order process to be able to continue extender installation._x000D_ o Technician must be required to capture customer approval as required (ex: collect T&C agreement, review pricing)_x000D_ o Technicians should be able to install as many extenders as needed within one session._x000D_ o System should allow auto-discovery of extenders. If auto-discovery fails or is delayed (value for delayed must be configurable by the business), system should allow the technician to scan the equipment being installed._x000D_ o Based on triggering of adding EWCS, all appropriate systems will be updated with the added service and equipment._x000D_ o Any scenarios where the technician has to await a response to continue in the flow must support a business defined SLA_x000D_ _x000D_ Other Guidance:_x000D_ _x000D_ - Want the process between both enabler and BSSe to be the same or better on BSSe_x000D_ - Legacy Vision: _x000D_ o Tech scans device_x000D_ o Notifies apps of adding the extender service to the account_x000D_ o Scan, Plug, Pair and go for technician…triggers activation, ordering (if needed), billing, global supply chain, nimbus_x000D_              -Display price screen isn’t necessarily ordering screen it’s notification to customer of pricing change, T&C, and documenting customers agreement_x000D_              - Nimbus allow for the customer to activate the extenders even before EWCS is ordered/assigned to the customer_x000D_ o Technician can add EWCS by either scanning the extender _x000D_              -Scan ties the extender Serial Number with the account number (For asset tracking and activation)_x000D_              - Need to define expected business response times_x000D_ - Scenario and BSSe vision: Tech arrives at job (Gateway only on the order)(big house need extenders)_x000D_ o Tell Atlas what doing (add extenders)( this triggers flow for T&C, pricing, T&C, signature (whatever is necessary for the add-on)_x000D_ o Tech plugs in extender and network auto-discovers extender_x000D_  On successful auto-discovery, the extenders will activate and tech continues with install_x000D_ o If auto-discovery doesn’t happen within a configurable period of time (ex. 30 sec), technician is able to scan the equipment and system will tie the extender to the account_x000D_ o Atlas flow adding EWCS should support adding multiple extenders within one flow_x000D_ - 4 scenarios_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on existing order_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on repair order_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on install and need to add extender_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on repair and needed a helper ticket_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ Note:  QB 2214 is making these FTI redesign changes to the current stack, and the Architecture Council has approved current stack work. If auto-discovery fails or is delayed (value for delayed must be configurable by the business), system should allow the technician to scan the equipment being installed._x000D_ o Based on triggering of adding EWCS, all appropriate systems will be updated with the added service and equipment._x000D_ o Any scenarios where the technician has to await a response to continue in the flow must support a business defined SLA_x000D_ _x000D_ Other Guidance:_x000D_ _x000D_ - Want the process between both enabler and BSSe to be the same or better on BSSe_x000D_ - Legacy Vision: _x000D_ o Tech scans device_x000D_ o Notifies apps of adding the extender service to the account_x000D_ o Scan, Plug, Pair and go for technician…triggers activation, ordering (if needed), billing, global supply chain, nimbus_x000D_              -Display price screen isn’t necessarily ordering screen it’s notification to customer of pricing change, T&C, and documenting customers agreement_x000D_              - Nimbus allow for the customer to activate the extenders even before EWCS is ordered/assigned to the customer_x000D_ o Technician can add EWCS by either scanning the extender _x000D_              -Scan ties the extender Serial Number with the account number (For asset tracking and activation)_x000D_              - Need to define expected business response times_x000D_ - Scenario and BSSe vision: Tech arrives at job (Gateway only on the order)(big house need extenders)_x000D_ o Tell Atlas what doing (add extenders)( this triggers flow for T&C, pricing, T&C, signature (whatever is necessary for the add-on)_x000D_ o Tech plugs in extender and network auto-discovers extender_x000D_  On successful auto-discovery, the extenders will activate and tech continues with install_x000D_ o If auto-discovery doesn’t happen within a configurable period of time (ex. 30 sec), technician is able to scan the equipment and system will tie the extender to the account_x000D_ o Atlas flow adding EWCS should support adding multiple extenders within one flow_x000D_ - 4 scenarios_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on existing order_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on repair order_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on install and need to add extender_x000D_ o Tech dispatched on repair and needed a helper ticket_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ Note:  QB 2214 is making these FTI redesign changes to the current stack, and the Architecture Council has approved current stack work.   Moving forward with BSSe changes.   _x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers(BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Types of Offers/Discounts:  _x000D_   - Cross-Sell (X% off mobility bill when adding Fiber)_x000D_   - Save_x000D_   - HBO Max included - offer + all necessary requirements to enable access to HBOMax access using broadband ID.  Configurable to be added or removed from plans based on business need_x000D_   - Access 2.0 discount_x000D_   - Employee discount_x000D_   - Retiree discount_x000D_   - $ off for X months_x000D_   - Autopay discount_x000D_   - Reward Card_x000D_   - Affordable Connectivity Plan discount_x000D_   - Ability to include or exclude device fees and taxes and fees in plan price_x000D_  _x000D_  Configurable setup for how business wants to bundle products.  Strategy can change in the future and need configurability to be able to change the various kits and products._x000D_  _x000D_  The assumption is leverage current BAU customer repositories for via TDATA/TDICE then to enable prioritization, personalization of the offers._x000D_  _x000D_  CLARIFICATION:   How does BSSe LIst #8 differ from #69 and #154?_x000D_  _x000D_  Per Sid (ss582h):  _x000D_  _x000D_  These 3 initiatives need to remain separate since I see these as three distinct items:_x000D_  _x000D_  BSSe List #8: Personalization as a service is Fiber MVP and intended for the scope of work that would expand smart recommendations into Fiber BSSe. Full tech installation will require a configurable fee that will be charged to the customer:_x000D_ • Fee can be configured at $0 so there is no charge to the customer_x000D_ • Fees can be configurable to be charged or taxed differently by state or location. _x000D_ • Fee should have ability to be waived by agents pre and post installation. Configurable to require Manager approval_x000D_ • Fee will be a separate charge within the cart._x000D_ Human assist channels will disclose charge at POS._x000D_ • Fee and installation promotions must be configurable to waive FTI charges with eligible promotions_x000D_ • Fees and installation promotions must be configurable based on customer type /indicator (upgrades, ADSL migration and future target migration customers) _x000D_ • Fees and installation charges must be created with ability to include time frame, limited number of promotion redemptions, and location (State, DMA, etc.) based._x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers(BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Should this dispatch on demand fee be in addition to one time tech install fee? – The business would like the Dispatch on Demand fee to be a configurable fee that can be added to customers that are eligible for CSI but refuse and request a FTI.  The dispatch on demand will be charged in place of the FTI fee.  There should not be a situation where both fees are required._x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(BB 4):_x000D_ (LC) Allowing technicians to add service / equipment to orders is part of fulfillment process and should be integrated with RTI for shopping and order submission at or after the time of install.  Added to Fulfillment support for FTI epic.

Pioritize to customers:_x000D_  - Plans_x000D_  - Offers_x000D_  - Discounts_x000D_ _x000D_ The assumption is leverage current BAU customer repositories for via TDATA/TDICE then to enable prioritization, personalization of the offers._x000D_ _x000D_ CLARIFICATION:   How does BSSe LIst #8 differ from #69 and #154?_x000D_ _x000D_ Per Sid (ss582h):  _x000D_ _x000D_ These 3 initiatives need to remain separate since I see these as three distinct items:_x000D_ _x000D_ BSSe List #8: Personalization as a service is Fiber MVP and intended for the scope of work that would expand smart recommendations into Fiber BSSe. This includes personalized content personalization on myaccount cards in various scenarios like upgrades, migrations, add-on recommendations. We would take additional inputs like data usage, plan history, tenure, etc to customize the suggestion provided to the customer. This has been done for Mobility and looking to be expanded for BAU Broadband and eventually would need to be included for Fiber BSSe._x000D_ _x000D_ BSSe List #69: IDR 257 should link to this ID and cover the Top Offers integration._x000D_ _x000D_ BSSe List #154: This should remain as the manual cross sell scope that has been slotted for Fiber CI. I am working with Ed Brown on the use cases to scope this for IDR 225. There will be cross sell offers that should be displayed in top offers so there is a dependency on this effort with 69 but I think it should remain separate since we are still evaluating the cross sell approach across BSSe and legacy platforms._x000D_

Supply Chain - Enhance Procurement logic to support VAR kitting and interfaces for returns and rekitting.  Leilani Camacho - Enhance the new interfaces we've created in Supply chain to better support the new VAR (kitting partner) and to support returns, refurbishment, and rekitting._x000D_ 7/18 Questions from Architecture & Corresponding responses from Product:_x000D_ Refurbishment and kitting roadmap -_x000D_ RL warehouse is a vendor, as all warehouses in SC are vendors._x000D_ AIA RL part 2 was shared to help with the details of what is needed._x000D_ Most of this will be SC backend process._x000D_ VAR means Value added reseller - these are purchase models and not all purchase models are supported by the vendors - thats a needed enhancement._x000D_ The refurbishement happens in RL and the vendor will have to reflash the firmware or eSIM, as needed. Another needed enhancement._x000D_ WMS (warehouse mgmt system) will need to build interfaces with the kitting VAR so that once we reach a certain stock level of refurbished equip, transportation planning can pick up the load and carry it to the VAR for kitting and then transport to the forward DC for outbound shipment to consumer (back into production).

Copyright Infringement - Supports AT&T legal mandate to address copyright infringement compliance.      Epic Content, per Meghna Sathe (BDO) - AT&T is legally required to address Copyright Infringement compliance any time we provide Internet service. This epic supports implementation of a Copyright Infringement Program for Wireless Broadband. WBB copyright infringement logic will be patterned like the Wireline Broadband program.   High level overview of the program-  1. The copyright holder notifies AT&T of what they believe to be an infringement and provides us the infringing file name and file type, the IP address and port from which that file was retrieved, and the timestamp of the event.  2. We look up the account owner based on the IP address and timestamp. No existing capability to get IP addresses for mobility, could be built but could affect timing due to complexity. Mobility has a pass from legal and does not have a copyright program. Possible vendor impact(Eco)  3. We then notify the account owner identified in step 2. Customer notifications will be sent via Salesforce CLM.  4. If infringements continue to occur, the policy committee makes decisions regarding suspension or termination of the account    C1: As AT&T, I need the ability to address copyright infringement every time we provide internet access.   C2: As a customer, I need the ability to be notified of copyright infringement and next steps I need to take.  Note:  Work is starting in PI 11 and may extend to PI 12/PI 13.  Confirmed with Bill Waugh, Architecture there will be minor, incremental scope for Fiber CI 1 based on supporting systems, but the majority we will be able to reuse from Internet Air.

Copyright Infringement - Supports AT&T legal mandate to address copyright infringement compliance.      Epic Content, per Meghna Sathe (BDO) - AT&T is legally required to address Copyright Infringement compliance any time we provide Internet service. This epic supports implementation of a Copyright Infringement Program for Wireless Broadband. WBB copyright infringement logic will be patterned like the Wireline Broadband program.   High level overview of the program-  1. The copyright holder notifies AT&T of what they believe to be an infringement and provides us the infringing file name and file type, the IP address and port from which that file was retrieved, and the timestamp of the event.  2. We look up the account owner based on the IP address and timestamp. No existing capability to get IP addresses for mobility, could be built but could affect timing due to complexity. Mobility has a pass from legal and does not have a copyright program. Possible vendor impact(Eco)  3. We then notify the account owner identified in step 2. Customer notifications will be sent via Salesforce CLM.  4. If infringements continue to occur, the policy committee makes decisions regarding suspension or termination of the account    C1: As AT&T, I need the ability to address copyright infringement every time we provide internet access. AMENDED SCOPE:   Effective 3Q2022, the CPE strategy for Fiber CI has been modified to no longer offer the CGW450 device and Wireless Back-up as an add-on in the Converged Kit.  The business will want Wireless Backup at Fiber MVP.   While the CGW450 will NOT be offered as we know it today, there will be a new TBD device name.    _x000D_ _x000D_ Converged Kit:  Bundle price for wireless back-up, boosters & advanced security._x005F_x000D__x000D_ Requires CGW450 hub and 4981 boostersArchitecture questions: _x000D_ 1. Need to understand how returns will be supported for non-working CGW, due to 5G signal issue, or defective device as part of a bundle (EWCS). Do we need to return both devices?  Will this be handled as part of an M&P process?_x000D_ 2. What work in Oracle/Supply Chain should we consider from a solution/Development for returns_x000D_ 3. From a Converged Kit/Bundle – do we need a single SKU for CGW and EWCS, or Oracle Bill of materials SKU?_x000D_ 4. How do we support CGW and EWCS FW updates (need discussion with the WBB for Fiber as how provisioning and how it is going to be implemented? _x000D_ 5. Need clarity on supporting fallouts. Need fallout management support from each area of the business_x000D_ 6. How do we handle FTI for Converged Kit (CGW and EWCS orders)? _x000D_ _x000D_ Product Answers below: _x000D_ Converged Kit is dependent on the full build out of WBB (Wireless Broadband) functionality. It is assumed that the development of AIA can be leveraged for WBB. However, there are areas to where we would need to build WBB to support usage and qualification process. _x000D_ Wireless Backup (CGW device) service will ONLY be available as part of the Converged Kit and will not be available as a stand-alone/bolt-on service. Channel availability is only consumer.  _x000D_ • We will sell Fiber on BSSe with different flavor offers  _x000D_ • Standard fiber customer will get the BGW320_x000D_ • Premium fiber is the Converged Kit (includes ISA, and WBB (Wireless Back Up) CGW450, with EWCS). It is a bundled experience current pricing $25. Customer without the EWCS will continue to be billed the $25. _x000D_ • There will be instances that customer with the Premium offer (Converged Kit) may or may NOT require an extender (EWCS) depending on the customer’s situation. There should be a process to determine how many boosters are required.  NOTE: A disclosure will be required to support the grouping for these items. Marketing to confirm how to market without EWCS if found customer doesn’t need it.  _x000D_ • EWCS and CGW will be handled in Oracle with its own serial# and SKU. They need to be configurable. _x000D_ • For EWCS, if we are shipping out 3 or 4 devices. The expectation from the Supply chain side is that it will have 3- or 4-line items. _x000D_ • WBB/CGW will be pulled out of Supply Chain that includes the NSM ranges that were set up with Mobility.  This will be part of the pre-provisioning process.  This will be carried throughout the systems. Set up of the inventory will have the serial number, attributes including the ranges._x000D_ • SKU processing will require Premium Fiber (CGW450) SKU and for each EWCS (SKUs). ISA (Internet Security Advanced) added to the offer and will follow the same provisioning process in place today. _x000D_ • We will use the same SKU for CGW450 that is currently use for AIA vs Fiber from a SKU perspective will be the same. _x000D_ • NOTE: There are plans in the future that they are going to introduce a new CGW with a newer technology that maybe used for Converged kit in the future. For now, we will use the same SKU that AIA is using today for the Premium Fiber (Converged Kit) _x000D_ Return process: _x000D_ • Customer must return all devices when canceling Converged Kit_x000D_ • If it’s a Tech install, there will be some expectation – TBD on process_x000D_ • NOTE: Per Jacqui for CI changing equipment based on services ordered for added or removed is not supported on an existing customer for Converged Kit. For CI, if they cancel the Converged Kit, they will keep the CGW, and if they don’t retain the EWCS separate, then they can return it. _x000D_ • For future BSSe effort, we need to add return, and change equipment as part of the Fiber MVP. With the restriction, if you don’t order WBB with Converged kit, that means you can’t add later. Customer will not be downgraded to BGW. They will have to keep their CGW.  _x000D_ Qualification Process: _x000D_ 1. Not everyone will qualify for WBB. Customer signing up with the Premium offer must go to through a qualification similar to AIA. The qualification process will not be strict as what AIA’s qualification process for WBB on fiber. Qualification will have different criteria. WBB doesn’t have to have a great connection as per Amy Hollister. _x000D_ 2. As part of the WBB installation process, is to check the wireless back up signal. If the signal is low, part of the qualification process includes a prediction we assumed that the customer has strong & enough signal to where they can use the WBB. If we find something in the installation process that FTI and customer cannot find adequate signal, then the customer will be given two options _x000D_ • Cancel the premium, and have standard fiber  _x000D_ • Keep the Premium experience, bill the premium charge, but will not get the WBB (not offering the discount rate). _x000D_ NOTE: As part of the order flow process, there will be disclaimers around the WBB services regarding predicting adequate signal.  _x000D_ Example: There are instances in most of the new homes that the fiber jack is tucked in a closet or basement, which then causes wireless signal to be very poor. Hence, not get good signal.  Per Amy, it needs to be check as part of the install process. For CSI, there will be a way in the process that checks the wireless signal. For FTI, they will need to follow the M&P, and or will require the signal strength to successfully do the install. _x000D_ Support for EWCS/Booster (Extended WiFi Coverage Service) from an FTI perspective_x000D_ • Whatever process we have today to install the booster from an FTI perspective will be followed per Jacqui and Bharat_x000D_ • Tech guy carries the device – will have this taken care of in supply chain. Per Leilani, she included in her scope – “The ability for the FT to use the equipment that is at the site to make sure it is working, if the transaction started out as a CSI”_x000D_ Provisioning of WBB _x000D_ • SIM card will be preinstalled in the device. Similar to how AIA is provisioned. It is part of the account order set up where the SIM is provisioned. The customer will go to the installation process, which will have checkpoint to make sure the device is getting adequate signal in order to activate the WBB. _x000D_ Are AIA and WBB both are fix - Yes_x000D_ • If WBB is the only service, the customer will be provided recommendation where to place the device in order to get the best signal. For new install, it will be part of the new fiber M&Ps to choose the best location to get better signal. Some of the places we install fiber today, cannot be beneficial for wireless signal._x000D_ Customer inventory would need to know when devices are shipped to the customer and should know what is in the customer premise. Per Jacqui, Supply Chain is supposed to do an immediate update to the customer’s account. _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_

RG Communication Protocol (TR-069 vs. USP) on New (BSSe) and Old (Enabler) Stacks - For BSSe, we are implementing USP, which is the evolution of the RG communication protocol, allowing the systems to communicate more quickly and in an asynchronous method.  That is necessary for future cases like OTT.   However, the BGW320 currently communicates using TR-069.   Decision Point - do we implement TR-069 on BSSe so that it can continue to communicate to the BGW320 when we launch Fiber OR do we upgrade the BGW320 to support USP?   The latter would require enhancements to Enabler.  The BGW cannot run both communication protocols.

Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Installation Type:   Customers should be informed that standard Full Tech Install includes 2 ethernet connects (amount of included ethernet connects configurable).   Additional ethernet connections can be added to the order.  Fee is configurable and will be set as._x000D_ _x000D_ During the buy flow, the customer should have the option to add additional ethernet wiring (Ethernet ports) in their home.  While the current plan is to offer 2 included with FTI (Configurable), the customer may want an additional ethernet connection(s) in their home so they can have a wired connection to the internet in their office, etc.  The customer may choose to have an additional 1- X (min/max configurable) ethernet ports added and these will be installed at the time of the FTI and the fees associated with it should be configurable with the ability to make it $0.  This information should flow to the tech who is installing the service so they can be aware of how much time to set aside for the installation. _x000D_ _x000D_ Fee handling is still under discussion._x000D_ _x000D_ The Field Technician will have the ability to submit a buried service request via Atlas following the BAU process today.  If Atlas fails, they would contact their support center (called ERC) and that center agent has the ability to manually create the ticket.

Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Lifecycle (Christine Feghali) - Save and retrieve cart (omni-channel, enables SOFIS, etc.)_x000D_ _x000D_ Title: Save and Retrieve Cart_x000D_ Description: As a BSSe customer, I need to be able to save a cart in any channel and retrieve and make changes to it in any channel. As a human assist agent, I need to be able to save a cart on behalf of a customer and retrieve and make changes to a cart that has been previously saved. _x000D_ _x000D_ Be able to start in Digital and end in Retail, for example w/out having to start over with the cart.   Likely a use case experienced more on the Wireless side.   Want this functionality for Fiber CI due to higher volumes anticipated._x000D_ _x000D_ 5/13/22 - (Brian Bland) - Per discussion with Leigh Mahindru, BOPIS is out of scope for Fiber CI, so removing from scope.   No IDR entry needed for this item as it's all AIA reuse now.

Add customer type indicator: _x005F_x000D__x000D_  - Employee_x005F_x000D__x000D_  - Retiree_x000D_ Clarifications per Chris Tupy _x000D_ 1. Currently Employee/Retiree enrollment is done through Digital Employee Portal (DEP).  Is this expectation to start selling Employee/Retiree service in all AT&T sales channels? – No.  I would also expect that the current Employee Discount (EDP) team  would need to be set up with profiles that allow for adding/removing/managing the employee accounts. _x000D_ _x000D_ 2. Do Employee/Retiree need separate identifiers or single identifier for both?  - I would expect we might want possibly 3 – Employee Management, Employee – Non-Management and Retiree.   I do not think we should box ourselves in like Enabler did where we were forced to have only a few account types.  The values should be more configurable._x000D_ _x000D_ 3. It sounds like no specific plans for Empl/Ret just differrent rates and maybe not eligible for some consumer plans?  Things like description, Rev & Tax split, T&C, etc. are common for Consumer and Empl/Ret?  This will drive catalog set up.  – There will be pricing changes – including options beyond just the offer.  Possibly to addon’s, equipment, etc.   Additionally there could be some implications/change to messaging.  However, other items like T&Cs, shipping, installation, etc. should not change.   _x000D_ _x000D_ Does the EDP team currently have profiles that allow adding/removing/managing employee discounts?  If so, are they using OPUS?_x000D_ EDP team does have a profile that allows agents to update account subtype.  Additionally, they can add and remove the employee promotions.  I believe that they currently use CRM/OMS not OPUS.
