AMENDED SCOPE:   Effective 3Q2022, the CPE strategy for Fiber CI has been modified to no longer offer the CGW450 device and Wireless Back-up as an add-on in the Converged Kit.  The business will want Wireless Backup at Fiber MVP.   While the CGW450 will NOT be offered as we know it today, there will be a new TBD device name.    _x000D_ _x000D_ Converged Kits Offers/Promotions:_x005F_x000D__x000D_ o  Multiple bundle variants which combine multiple bolt-on features and equipment types into one feature at one MRC_x005F_x000D__x000D_ o  Bundle A (Converged Kit for Fiber): _x005F_x000D__x000D_      - Converged Gateway, compatible Wi-Fi Extenders (0-n where n is configurable)_x005F_x000D__x000D_      - Advanced Security_x005F_x000D__x000D_      - 5G Wireless Backup_x005F_x000D__x000D_      - current projected price point of $25/month (taxes/fees NOT included).  _x005F_x000D__x000D_      - Bundle A price points configurable by customer type_x005F_x000D__x000D_ o  Bundle B (Converged Kit for WBB): _x005F_x000D__x000D_      - Compatible Wi-Fi Extenders and Advanced Security (this is the only option available to 5G Wireless Broadband, but may also be available to other AT&T customers)_x005F_x000D__x000D_      - Current projected price point of $15/month (taxes/fees NOT included)_x005F_x000D__x000D_      - Bundle B price points configurable by customer type_x005F_x000D__x000D_ o  Ability for customer to add incremental Boosters beyond the Converged Kit limit for a low monthly bolt-on cost (incremental to Converged Kit cost)_x000D_ _x000D_ Configurable setup for how business wants to bundle products.  Strategy can change in the future and need configurability to be able to change the various kits and products._x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture questions: _x000D_ 1. Need to understand how returns will be supported for non-working CGW, due to 5G signal issue, or defective device as part of a bundle (EWCS). Do we need to return both devices?  Will this be handled as part of an M&P process?_x000D_ 2. What work in Oracle/Supply Chain should we consider from a solution/Development for returns_x000D_ 3. From a Converged Kit/Bundle – do we need a single SKU for CGW and EWCS, or Oracle Bill of materials SKU?_x000D_ 4. How do we support CGW and EWCS FW updates (need discussion with the WBB for Fiber as how provisioning and how it is going to be implemented? _x000D_ 5. Need clarity on supporting fallouts. Need fallout management support from each area of the business_x000D_ 6. How do we handle FTI for Converged Kit (CGW and EWCS orders)? _x000D_ _x000D_ Product Answers below: _x000D_ Converged Kit is dependent on the full build out of WBB (Wireless Broadband) functionality. It is assumed that the development of AIA can be leveraged for WBB. However, there are areas to where we would need to build WBB to support usage and qualification process. _x000D_ Wireless Backup (CGW device) service will ONLY be available as part of the Converged Kit and will not be available as a stand-alone/bolt-on service. Channel availability is only consumer.  _x000D_ • We will sell Fiber on BSSe with different flavor offers  _x000D_ • Standard fiber customer will get the BGW320_x000D_ • Premium fiber is the Converged Kit (includes ISA, and WBB (Wireless Back Up) CGW450, with EWCS). It is a bundled experience current pricing $25. Customer without the EWCS will continue to be billed the $25. _x000D_ • There will be instances that customer with the Premium offer (Converged Kit) may or may NOT require an extender (EWCS) depending on the customer’s situation. There should be a process to determine how many boosters are required.  NOTE: A disclosure will be required to support the grouping for these items. Marketing to confirm how to market without EWCS if found customer doesn’t need it.  _x000D_ • EWCS and CGW will be handled in Oracle with its own serial# and SKU. They need to be configurable. _x000D_ • For EWCS, if we are shipping out 3 or 4 devices. The expectation from the Supply chain side is that it will have 3- or 4-line items. _x000D_ • WBB/CGW will be pulled out of Supply Chain that includes the NSM ranges that were set up with Mobility.  This will be part of the pre-provisioning process.  This will be carried throughout the systems. Set up of the inventory will have the serial number, attributes including the ranges._x000D_ • SKU processing will require Premium Fiber (CGW450) SKU and for each EWCS (SKUs). ISA (Internet Security Advanced) added to the offer and will follow the same provisioning process in place today. _x000D_ • We will use the same SKU for CGW450 that is currently use for AIA vs Fiber from a SKU perspective will be the same. _x000D_ • NOTE: There are plans in the future that they are going to introduce a new CGW with a newer technology that maybe used for Converged kit in the future. For now, we will use the same SKU that AIA is using today for the Premium Fiber (Converged Kit) _x000D_ Return process: _x000D_ • Customer must return all devices when canceling Converged Kit_x000D_ • If it’s a Tech install, there will be some expectation – TBD on process_x000D_ • NOTE: Per Jacqui for CI changing equipment based on services ordered for added or removed is not supported on an existing customer for Converged Kit. For CI, if they cancel the Converged Kit, they will keep the CGW, and if they don’t retain the EWCS separate, then they can return it. _x000D_ • For future BSSe effort, we need to add return, and change equipment as part of the Fiber MVP. With the restriction, if you don’t order WBB with Converged kit, that means you can’t add later. Customer will not be downgraded to BGW. They will have to keep their CGW.  _x000D_ Qualification Process: _x000D_ 1. Not everyone will qualify for WBB. Customer signing up with the Premium offer must go to through a qualification similar to AIA. The qualification process will not be strict as what AIA’s qualification process for WBB on fiber. Qualification will have different criteria. WBB doesn’t have to have a great connection as per Amy Hollister. _x000D_ 2. As part of the WBB installation process, is to check the wireless back up signal. If the signal is low, part of the qualification process includes a prediction we assumed that the customer has strong & enough signal to where they can use the WBB. If we find something in the installation process that FTI and customer cannot find adequate signal, then the customer will be given two options _x000D_ • Cancel the premium, and have standard fiber  _x000D_ • Keep the Premium experience, bill the premium charge, but will not get the WBB (not offering the discount rate). _x000D_ NOTE: As part of the order flow process, there will be disclaimers around the WBB services regarding predicting adequate signal.  _x000D_ Example: There are instances in most of the new homes that the fiber jack is tucked in a closet or basement, which then causes wireless signal to be very poor. Hence, not get good signal.  Per Amy, it needs to be check as part of the install process. For CSI, there will be a way in the process that checks the wireless signal. For FTI, they will need to follow the M&P, and or will require the signal strength to successfully do the install. _x000D_ Support for EWCS/Booster (Extended WiFi Coverage Service) from an FTI perspective_x000D_ • Whatever process we have today to install the booster from an FTI perspective will be followed per Jacqui and Bharat_x000D_ • Tech guy carries the device – will have this taken care of in supply chain. Per Leilani, she included in her scope – “The ability for the FT to use the equipment that is at the site to make sure it is working, if the transaction started out as a CSI”_x000D_ Provisioning of WBB _x000D_ • SIM card will be preinstalled in the device. Similar to how AIA is provisioned. It is part of the account order set up where the SIM is provisioned. The customer will go to the installation process, which will have checkpoint to make sure the device is getting adequate signal in order to activate the WBB. _x000D_ Are AIA and WBB both are fix - Yes_x000D_ • If WBB is the only service, the customer will be provided recommendation where to place the device in order to get the best signal. For new install, it will be part of the new fiber M&Ps to choose the best location to get better signal. Some of the places we install fiber today, cannot be beneficial for wireless signal._x000D_ Customer inventory would need to know when devices are shipped to the customer and should know what is in the customer premise. Per Jacqui, Supply Chain is supposed to do an immediate update to the customer’s account. _x000D_

HomeTech Protection for Broadband (Jen Haig request)_x000D_ _x000D_ Problem Statement:_x000D_ Broadband Product is seeking solutions that provide customers with ways to support 3rd Party Devices that are connected to WiFi. Research shows that customers need additional support. Also, when they are dissatistifed with their WiFi connectivity experience, there is a correlating dissatisfaction with their ISP._x000D_ _x000D_ Simplified Description:  _x000D_ Build capability to sell the HomeTech Protection product in Broadband channels and to place it onto the Broadband bill.   This exists today in the Wireless channels biller.   _x000D_ _x000D_ Detailed Description:  AT&T currently sells HomeTech Protection in our Mobility channels. A customer must have a wireless account to sign-up, and the MRC of $25 appears on the wireless bill. This product provides the customer protection (insurance) for all their home technologies (TV, PC, connected devices) as well as 24/7 Tech Support for those 3rd Party Devices. There are currently ~25k broadband/WLS bundled customers that have this product. As Broadband Product seeks ways to better support customers with their 3rd Party Devices, we are seeking to allow the HTP product to be sold to stand-alone Broadband customers in Broadband channels. _x000D_ The vendor for the product is Asurion, so there will need to be an interface with Asurion to provide new orders and disconnects back and forth daily. We would want to make available to current Fiber customers on old stack, BSSE when it launches for Fiber, and copper customers on old stack…any BB customer._x000D_ _x000D_ QB 3466 is carrying for current stack changes to support.   QB 3929 is for new stack.   _x000D_ _x000D_ Scope Clarification Discussion 5/10/22:_x000D_ _x000D_ 1. How can the product be ordered?   Any channel that sells broadband that we are planning to enable by Fiber CI (currently in scope – online, centers, retail, DAP-digital acquisition partners)_x000D_ 2. Needs to be available as a product in the same sales flows (NOT like Connect Tech)_x000D_ 3. Use Case – customer calls in for support on a connected device such as a Nest Cam.   We offer to sell them HomeTech Protection.  If the customer signs up, we transfer them to Asurion for support. _x000D_ 4. HomeTech Protection product would be added under the Billing account (BAN)_x000D_ 5. Product is available to both AIA and Fiber_x000D_ 6. IMPORTANT:   If a customer has a wireless account and has signed up for HomeTech Protection, the coverage is at the address level regardless of the # of products on the account.   So if the customer who has wireless and is ordering fiber already has HomeTech, do NOT present HomeTech Protection as a sales option (Customer Graph check)_x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Questions:_x000D_ Need to add Vanita Sagar as a peer Architect/SME._x000D_ Need to validate what is being asked for in terms of scope/requirements (Items discussed)?_x000D_ Need to understand what will be happening with the Legacy project?_x000D_ Validate assumption that any channel that sells Fiber BB will be in scope?_x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response:_x000D_ If a customer already has the product, active for the Service Address, suppress the offer in the Fiber flows._x000D_ Need to modify legacy mobility flow to suppress ability to order the product if the customer has the product through the Fiber offer._x000D_ Need to have capability for any existing customer to order or migrate._x000D_ Any channel that sells Fiber or AT&T Internet Air will be in-scope._x000D_ _x000D_ Believe PI14 is most likely opportunity to target the LegacyBB effort on Enabler platform.  If BB Legacy project is implemented, migration will be required._x000D_ _x000D_ Do want the IVR to recognize call intent when the customer has the product so they can be routed directly to the 3rd party provider, Asurion.  If not recognized call would route to Tech Care who could transfer the call to the vendor._x000D_ _x000D_ We are already sending files to Asurion for Mobility customers, would also need to send files for Fiber to Asurion.  (Assume the same format with exception of CTN or perhaps BAN)._x000D_ Need to consume information from Asurion where customer already has the product for the Service Address to suppress offer._x000D_ We receive claim information from Asurion today, this should continue BAU.

As Broadband Product seeks ways to better support customers with their 3rd Party Devices, we are seeking to allow the HTP product to be sold to stand-alone Broadband customers in Broadband channels. _x000D_ The vendor for the product is Asurion, so there will need to be an interface with Asurion to provide new orders and disconnects back and forth daily. We would want to make available to current Fiber customers on old stack, BSSE when it launches for Fiber, and copper customers on old stack…any BB customer._x000D_ _x000D_ QB 3466 is carrying for current stack changes to support.   QB 3929 is for new stack.   _x000D_ _x000D_ Scope Clarification Discussion 5/10/22:_x000D_ _x000D_ 1. How can the product be ordered?   Any channel that sells broadband that we are planning to enable by Fiber CI (currently in scope – online, centers, retail, DAP-digital acquisition partners)_x000D_ 2. Needs to be available as a product in the same sales flows (NOT like Connect Tech)_x000D_ 3. Use Case – customer calls in for support on a connected device such as a Nest Cam.   We offer to sell them HomeTech Protection.  If the customer signs up, we transfer them to Asurion for support. _x000D_ 4. HomeTech Protection product would be added under the Billing account (BAN)_x000D_ 5. Product is available to both AIA and Fiber_x000D_ 6. IMPORTANT:   If a customer has a wireless account and has signed up for HomeTech Protection, the coverage is at the address level regardless of the # of products on the account.   So if the customer who has wireless and is ordering fiber already has HomeTech, do NOT present HomeTech Protection as a sales option (Customer Graph check)_x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Questions:_x000D_ Need to add Vanita Sagar as a peer Architect/SME._x000D_ Need to validate what is being asked for in terms of scope/requirements (Items discussed)?_x000D_ Need to understand what will be happening with the Legacy project?_x000D_ Validate assumption that any channel that sells Fiber BB will be in scope?_x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response:_x000D_ If a customer already has the product, active for the Service Address, suppress the offer in the Fiber flows._x000D_ Need to modify legacy mobility flow to suppress ability to order the product if the customer has the product through the Fiber offer._x000D_ Need to have capability for any existing customer to order or migrate._x000D_ Any channel that sells Fiber or AT&T Internet Air will be in-scope._x000D_ _x000D_ Believe PI14 is most likely opportunity to target the LegacyBB effort on Enabler platform.  If BB Legacy project is implemented, migration will be required._x000D_ _x000D_ Do want the IVR to recognize call intent when the customer has the product so they can be routed directly to the 3rd party provider, Asurion.  If not recognized call would route to Tech Care who could transfer the call to the vendor._x000D_ _x000D_ We are already sending files to Asurion for Mobility customers, would also need to send files for Fiber to Asurion.  (Assume the same format with exception of CTN or perhaps BAN)._x000D_ Need to consume information from Asurion where customer already has the product for the Service Address to suppress offer._x000D_ We receive claim information from Asurion today, this should continue BAU.

his epic is intended to align BSSe to operational capabilities currently used for Voluntary Suspensions for mobility customers. This document combines foundational attributes of previously submitted AIA epics (referenced in the dependencies) which includes shared capabilities as well as unique Wireless capabilities for voluntary suspensions. This will support the expansion of the Wireless product onto the BSSe platform.   This will support Military Suspension.     For Military deployment, MRCs will bill at 0$ and remaining installment balance will be credited to zero (applicable after device financing is available)     C1: Military Cancels, Hold CTN(s), Suspends     Product Owner POV     As a business product owner, I need to be able to add, remove, and edit reasons for voluntary suspensions, military cancellations, and business rules, based on the available suspension reason for Wireless. JE - General configurable suspend and restore elements (All content, Headers, sub headers, error messages, CTA’s, Pricing/T&Cs if applicable, etc.) in the online self-service experience need to be daily releasable.     Customer POV     As an existing customer, I want to be able to place my wireless service (voice, data and messaging) on hold voluntarily for a specific reason according to appropriate business rules through my online account [DIGITAL] or using an Agent [HUMAN ASSIST]. I also need to be able to cancel my service for verified military and retain my CTN(s) by submitting appropriate documentation via a given URL (Currently https://www.att.com/activedeployment).          Military Cancels     C1AC10: Military Verified Cancel     As an existing Wireless customer on active duty, I need the ability to my cancel services (e.g., data, voice, messaging) on my impacted CTN (s) after I submit my military orders per the Service Members Civil Relief Act.     C1AC10a: Ability to cancel service (voice, data, and messaging) for military deployment.     C1AC10b: Ability to cancel up to 30 days in advance.     C1AC10c: Ability to hold CTN(s) and BAN for a maximum of 39 months.     C1AC10d: Ability to allow AT&T to release my CTN after 39 months plus 1 day if I do not resume service or reapply by submitting new orders within 39 months.     C1AC10e: Ability to elect not to hold my CTN(s) and release my CTN(s) per existing policy.     C1AC20d: Ability to prorate or not prorate MRCs, based on business rules, when CTN(s) are cancelled for Military.       C1AC10f: If all CTNs are cancelled, I will continue to receive a bill until there are no financial activities, past due, etc. for more than 30 days - including when a bill nets to $0.     C1AC10g: If one or more CTNs on the BAN remain active or suspended, I will continue to receive a bill.     C1AC10h: Ability to receive notification prior to cancellation of add-ons which are not available to resume. (e.g., Roadside assistance, if available).     C1AC20: Ability to provide customer with online access to their account (via access ID, user id, etc.) during the timeframe we are holding their CTN(s).     C1AC20a: Ability to access account.     C1AC20b: Ability to update account/profile information.          C1AC30: I need the ability to resume my CTN(s) within the 39 months, if CTN(s) were held or within the aging period (if CTN(s) were not held) from the cancellation date.     C1AC30a: I need the ability to resume the CTN(s) on the same BAN as a simple ‘resume from cancel’ transaction.     C1AC30b: I need the ability to resume to my previous plan prior to my military cancellation.     C1AC30c: I need the ability to add the device protection plan I had prior to cancelling service.     C1AC30d: Ability to prorate or not prorate MRCs, based on business rules, when CTN(s) are resumed from cancel for Military.      C1AC30e: Ability to display on an immediate receipt or anniversary invoice, based on general bill format rules.???     C1AC30f: Ability to automatically resume add-ons upon CTN restoral (Based on add-on availability).     C1AC40: I need the ability to change my CTN(s), which were not held at cancellation, to held status if I am within the aging period.      C1AC50: I need the ability to extend my military cancellation status and continue to hold my CTN(s) and BAN.      C1AC50a: I need to be able to modify my cancellation status without having to resume service and re-cancel.     C1AC50b: Ability to re-verify deployment orders via an agent [HUMAN ASSIST] or online [DIGITAL]. Under what circumstances must the customer re-apply?      Note: follow Terminate Wireless Service Epic (QB#6477/SRT#975 1B; SRT 974 Cancel a Line 1A; Resume from Cancel (account) & Resume a line (CTN) SRT 1209 – phase 1b).          <Military verified cancel – Port out?>     C1AC60: As an agent [HUMAN ASSIST], I need to be able to cancel a customer’s Wireless service for military verified cancel.     C1AC70: As an existing wireless customer [DIGITAL], I need to be able to submit my deployment orders online via URL (currently https://www.att.com/activedeployment), by fax or with an agent.     C1AC80: Ability to receive a communication confirming my CTN status’.               Military Unverified Suspension     C1AC90: As an existing Wireless customer and active service person eligible for military suspension, I need the ability to voluntarily suspend my service for my military deployment, when I have not validated my service orders and deployment dates.     C2AC90a: Ability to suspend my service.     C2AC90b: Ability to receive a bill for the full MRC on my suspended line and any active CTN(s) on my account.     C2AC10d: Ability to change my voluntary military suspend status once I verify my military deployment status to military verified cancel.          C2: Stolen Suspend      C2AC10: As an existing customer, I need to be able to voluntarily suspend my CTN’s service (voice, data, and messaging) on my stolen device.     C2AC10a: Ability to blocklist device.     C2AC10b: Ability to be billed the full MRCs.     C2AC10c: Ability to receive a communication confirming stolen suspend and blocklisting status. Bill Message?          C2AC20: As an existing customer in Stolen suspend status, I need the ability to restore service (voice, data, and messaging) on my CTN via an agent [HUMAN ASSIST].     C2AC20a: Ability to restore from stolen suspend status if I recover or replace my device.     C2AC20b: Ability to remove device from blocklist.     C2AC20c: Ability to receive a communication confirming restoral and blocklisting removal.

Fiber customers are eligible to suspend their account given special circumstances.   Eligibility criteria for suspension:   - Existing AT&T Internet customers  - Service active for at least 30 days  -  Account in good standing with no past-due balance  Customers who suspend service:   - Suspend all AT&T Internet, Phone, and/or U-verse TV services with the request. Cannot suspend individual services.    - RC1 and Converted DIRECTV services can be suspended independently.  - Some Voluntary Suspend plans have a MRC.  - Promotions continue to count down and may expire.    - Promotional prices do not apply to the account.  Restrictions  - Do not place service on Voluntary Suspend more than twice per year (rolling 12-month period).      - If they claim that AT&T added it too early, or indicates other issues, allow the voluntary suspension.     - Override the maximum suspension times via Manager Override in special circumstances (at manager discretion only).  - Do not place orders to an account on Voluntary Suspend.        - Customers must reconnect service at the same location prior to placing an order.

Restrictions  - Do not place service on Voluntary Suspend more than twice per year (rolling 12-month period).      - If they claim that AT&T added it too early, or indicates other issues, allow the voluntary suspension.     - Override the maximum suspension times via Manager Override in special circumstances (at manager discretion only).  - Do not place orders to an account on Voluntary Suspend.        - Customers must reconnect service at the same location prior to placing an order.

AMENDED SCOPE:   Effective 3Q2022, the CPE strategy for Fiber CI has been modified to no longer offer the CGW450 device and Wireless Back-up as an add-on in the Converged Kit.  The business will want Wireless Backup at Fiber MVP.   While the CGW450 will NOT be offered as we know it today, there will be a new TBD device name.    _x000D_ _x000D_ Converged Kit Additions:_x000D_  - Contextual card to appear in Smart Home Manager (or its successor) which informs Converged Kit customers of the combined value of their chosen solution (equivalent to 'Your Internet Looks Great' proactive card, with enhanced messaging)_x000D_  - Proactive care experiences for Converged Kit customers, such as proactive detection of need to change placement of device for 5G Wireless Backup coverage_x000D_  - Automated post-purchase messaging and email should remind customer of their package features and how to access them, such as enabling Advanced Security and installing their new equipment_x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture questions: _x000D_ 1. Need to understand how returns will be supported for non-working CGW, due to 5G signal issue, or defective device as part of a bundle (EWCS). Do we need to return both devices?  Will this be handled as part of an M&P process?_x000D_ 2. What work in Oracle/Supply Chain should we consider from a solution/Development for returns_x000D_ 3. From a Converged Kit/Bundle – do we need a single SKU for CGW and EWCS, or Oracle Bill of materials SKU?_x000D_ 4. How do we support CGW and EWCS FW updates (need discussion with the WBB for Fiber as how provisioning and how it is going to be implemented? _x000D_ 5. Need clarity on supporting fallouts. Need fallout management support from each area of the business_x000D_ 6. How do we handle FTI for Converged Kit (CGW and EWCS orders)? _x000D_ _x000D_ Product Answers below: _x000D_ Converged Kit is dependent on the full build out of WBB (Wireless Broadband) functionality. It is assumed that the development of AIA can be leveraged for WBB. However, there are areas to where we would need to build WBB to support usage and qualification process. _x000D_ Wireless Backup (CGW device) service will ONLY be available as part of the Converged Kit and will not be available as a stand-alone/bolt-on service. Channel availability is only consumer.  _x000D_ • We will sell Fiber on BSSe with different flavor offers  _x000D_ • Standard fiber customer will get the BGW320_x000D_ • Premium fiber is the Converged Kit (includes ISA, and WBB (Wireless Back Up) CGW450, with EWCS). It is a bundled experience current pricing $25. Customer without the EWCS will continue to be billed the $25. _x000D_ • There will be instances that customer with the Premium offer (Converged Kit) may or may NOT require an extender (EWCS) depending on the customer’s situation. There should be a process to determine how many boosters are required.  NOTE: A disclosure will be required to support the grouping for these items. Marketing to confirm how to market without EWCS if found customer doesn’t need it.  _x000D_ • EWCS and CGW will be handled in Oracle with its own serial# and SKU. They need to be configurable. _x000D_ • For EWCS, if we are shipping out 3 or 4 devices. The expectation from the Supply chain side is that it will have 3- or 4-line items. _x000D_ • WBB/CGW will be pulled out of Supply Chain that includes the NSM ranges that were set up with Mobility.  This will be part of the pre-provisioning process.  This will be carried throughout the systems. Set up of the inventory will have the serial number, attributes including the ranges._x000D_ • SKU processing will require Premium Fiber (CGW450) SKU and for each EWCS (SKUs). ISA (Internet Security Advanced) added to the offer and will follow the same provisioning process in place today. _x000D_ • We will use the same SKU for CGW450 that is currently use for AIA vs Fiber from a SKU perspective will be the same. _x000D_ • NOTE: There are plans in the future that they are going to introduce a new CGW with a newer technology that maybe used for Converged kit in the future. For now, we will use the same SKU that AIA is using today for the Premium Fiber (Converged Kit) _x000D_ Return process: _x000D_ • Customer must return all devices when canceling Converged Kit_x000D_ • If it’s a Tech install, there will be some expectation – TBD on process_x000D_ • NOTE: Per Jacqui for CI changing equipment based on services ordered for added or removed is not supported on an existing customer for Converged Kit. For CI, if they cancel the Converged Kit, they will keep the CGW, and if they don’t retain the EWCS separate, then they can return it. _x000D_ • For future BSSe effort, we need to add return, and change equipment as part of the Fiber MVP. With the restriction, if you don’t order WBB with Converged kit, that means you can’t add later. Customer will not be downgraded to BGW. They will have to keep their CGW.  _x000D_ Qualification Process: _x000D_ 1. Not everyone will qualify for WBB. Customer signing up with the Premium offer must go to through a qualification similar to AIA. The qualification process will not be strict as what AIA’s qualification process for WBB on fiber. Qualification will have different criteria. WBB doesn’t have to have a great connection as per Amy Hollister. _x000D_ 2. As part of the WBB installation process, is to check the wireless back up signal. If the signal is low, part of the qualification process includes a prediction we assumed that the customer has strong & enough signal to where they can use the WBB. If we find something in the installation process that FTI and customer cannot find adequate signal, then the customer will be given two options _x000D_ • Cancel the premium, and have standard fiber  _x000D_ • Keep the Premium experience, bill the premium charge, but will not get the WBB (not offering the discount rate). _x000D_ NOTE: As part of the order flow process, there will be disclaimers around the WBB services regarding predicting adequate signal.  _x000D_ Example: There are instances in most of the new homes that the fiber jack is tucked in a closet or basement, which then causes wireless signal to be very poor. Hence, not get good signal.  Per Amy, it needs to be check as part of the install process. For CSI, there will be a way in the process that checks the wireless signal. For FTI, they will need to follow the M&P, and or will require the signal strength to successfully do the install. _x000D_ Support for EWCS/Booster (Extended WiFi Coverage Service) from an FTI perspective_x000D_ • Whatever process we have today to install the booster from an FTI perspective will be followed per Jacqui and Bharat_x000D_ • Tech guy carries the device – will have this taken care of in supply chain. Per Leilani, she included in her scope – “The ability for the FT to use the equipment that is at the site to make sure it is working, if the transaction started out as a CSI”_x000D_ Provisioning of WBB _x000D_ • SIM card will be preinstalled in the device. Similar to how AIA is provisioned. It is part of the account order set up where the SIM is provisioned. The customer will go to the installation process, which will have checkpoint to make sure the device is getting adequate signal in order to activate the WBB. _x000D_ Are AIA and WBB both are fix - Yes_x000D_ • If WBB is the only service, the customer will be provided recommendation where to place the device in order to get the best signal. For new install, it will be part of the new fiber M&Ps to choose the best location to get better signal. Some of the places we install fiber today, cannot be beneficial for wireless signal._x000D_ Customer inventory would need to know when devices are shipped to the customer and should know what is in the customer premise. Per Jacqui, Supply Chain is supposed to do an immediate update to the customer’s account. _x000D_

Include Dynamic IP Addressing. _x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Question(s): combined feedback for List # 109, 169._x000D_ What are we looking at for Real-time facility assighment?_x000D_ How is capacity planning going to be done?_x000D_ Any difference in how a CSI and FTI is handled?_x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response:_x000D_ Revised Detailed Desctiption:_x000D_ Installation Type:  Customers at an address which has a Fiber Jack and with Fiber present all the way to the Remote Gateway will qualify for Customer Self Install (CSI)._x000D_ Initial Feedback:_x000D_ Legacy stack has the "dual XGS" configuration due to the fact that XGS is a subtype of GPON.  We need to configure unique/distinct transport types, one for GPON to serve speed tiers below 1GB and XGSPON for speeds 1GB and above. Another point here is that we need to make sure that once a customer is provisioned on XGS, that customer stays on XGS for all speed tiers.  It also identifies the facilities to be used at time of order.  _x000D_ 7/12 Meeting Notes:_x000D_ There is a team looking at exhaust conditions for PFPs and a fallout process that creates a SHOTS ticket for C&E when capacity is exhausted._x000D_ -In BSSE we need to fix the subtype issue, created previously where XGS is a sub-type of GPON.  _x000D_ -Real-time facility assighment is needed to utilize the imbedded GPON capacity and therefore, 1gig and below speed tiers should be put on GPON.  Once the capacity is exhausted all assignments, regardless of speed tier should be made on XGS._x000D_ i. Identify capacity exhaust scenarios in the ordering system_x000D_ ii. Push out the provisioning date by configurable(~19) number of days_x000D_ iii. Issue an automated SHOTS(7838) ticket to resolve the capacity exhaust_x000D_ iv. Complete the order submission_x000D_ -This pertains to FTI only and assumes CSI will have facilities in place._x000D_ -Assume assigned OLT ports will support CSI._x000D_

Network service shall be provisioned prior to customer attempting activation.  Include Dynamic IP Addressing. _x000D_ _x000D_ Architecture Question(s): combined feedback for List # 109, 169._x000D_ What are we looking at for Real-time facility assighment?_x000D_ How is capacity planning going to be done?_x000D_ Any difference in how a CSI and FTI is handled?_x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response:_x000D_ Revised Detailed Desctiption:_x000D_ Installation Type:  Customers at an address which has a Fiber Jack and with Fiber present all the way to the Remote Gateway will qualify for Customer Self Install (CSI)._x000D_ Initial Feedback:_x000D_ Legacy stack has the "dual XGS" configuration due to the fact that XGS is a subtype of GPON.  We need to configure unique/distinct transport types, one for GPON to serve speed tiers below 1GB and XGSPON for speeds 1GB and above. Another point here is that we need to make sure that once a customer is provisioned on XGS, that customer stays on XGS for all speed tiers.  It also identifies the facilities to be used at time of order.  _x000D_ 7/12 Meeting Notes:_x000D_ There is a team looking at exhaust conditions for PFPs and a fallout process that creates a SHOTS ticket for C&E when capacity is exhausted._x000D_ -In BSSE we need to fix the subtype issue, created previously where XGS is a sub-type of GPON.  _x000D_ -Real-time facility assighment is needed to utilize the imbedded GPON capacity and therefore, 1gig and below speed tiers should be put on GPON.  Once the capacity is exhausted all assignments, regardless of speed tier should be made on XGS._x000D_ i. Identify capacity exhaust scenarios in the ordering system_x000D_ ii. Push out the provisioning date by configurable(~19) number of days_x000D_ iii. Issue an automated SHOTS(7838) ticket to resolve the capacity exhaust_x000D_ iv. Complete the order submission_x000D_ -This pertains to FTI only and assumes CSI will have facilities in place._x000D_ -Assume assigned OLT ports will support CSI._x000D_

Fiber: _x000D_ Technician notes from installation or repair activities should be visible to call center agents.  _x000D_ _x000D_ Recommendation to display the notes from the last three dispatches._x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers (BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Should this dispatch on demand fee be in addition to one time tech install fee? – The business would like the Dispatch on Demand fee to be a configurable fee that can be added to customers that are eligible for CSI but refuse and request a FTI.  The dispatch on demand will be charged in place of the FTI fee.  There should not be a situation where both fees are required._x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(BB 4):_x000D_ (LC) Allowing technicians to add service / equipment to orders is part of fulfillment process and should be integrated with RTI for shopping and order submission at or after the time of install.  Added to Fulfillment support for FTI epic.

Bus_Cap Component: Third party banner pass through to online buy flow with discounts (Costco, etc.) (CaaS - Commerce as a Service)
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Commerce as a Service (CaaS), as it relates to AT&T, is the ability for us to sell mobility service through a third party’s site.     For example, if you go to Cell Phones & Accessories  | Costco, and click on the blue banner for “Exclusive Rewards & Savings for Costco Members” – you’ll be directed to a buy flow that looks just AT&T’s BAU online buy flow, but we’re tracking that you came from Costco’s site and are able to give you exclusive benefits because you are a Costco member.     Costco is just one example of several of these CaaS relationships we have with 3rd parties.     3rd parties enabled with CaaS:    Costco  Sam's Club  BJ's Wholesale  AAFES  The Home Depot  Abt  Newegg  Belkin  Alaska Communications  Windstream    See attachment for example of what is offered at COSTCO.      The CaaS team is in the process of requesting a BYOD offer for these brands plus any new brands that are pitched.     Ensure that these customers are eligible for Signature/Appreciation Discounts

For example, if you go to Cell Phones & Accessories  | Costco, and click on the blue banner for “Exclusive Rewards & Savings for Costco Members” – you’ll be directed to a buy flow that looks just AT&T’s BAU online buy flow, but we’re tracking that you came from Costco’s site and are able to give you exclusive benefits because you are a Costco member.     Costco is just one example of several of these CaaS relationships we have with 3rd parties.     3rd parties enabled with CaaS:    Costco  Sam's Club  BJ's Wholesale  AAFES  The Home Depot  Abt  Newegg  Belkin  Alaska Communications  Windstream    See attachment for example of what is offered at COSTCO.      The CaaS team is in the process of requesting a BYOD offer for these brands plus any new brands that are pitched.     Ensure that these customers are eligible for Signature/Appreciation Discounts

Bus_Cap Component: TS: Failed CSI incl Drop Ship & Tech Install Appt
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Fiber Troubleshooting (Activation/Installation):_x000D_  - Call center agent to support service activation (customer and technician)_x000D_  - Customers who fail CSI installation will have the ability to call into customer care and change from a CSI to an FTI.  Customer will be charged the FTI Fee. _x000D_  - Fiber Customer and agent will have access to a Tech Tracker feature that provides status of full tech installation (due date, technician status etc.)_x000D_ _x000D_ Architect questions and PO answers (BB 5): _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. Should the business_x000D_ a. prefer onetime payment during the offer?  – The business would like for the payment (assuming you are referring to adding services: Adv Sec, Extended Wi-Fi Coverage) to be configurable to be either a one-time payment or a configurable number of installment payments._x000D_ <Vas> I am talking about FTI one time Fee during the point of sale. Not the add-ons. Looks like you answered in 1b. and also indicated that on demand FTI fee is charged in place of one time FTI Fee not charge them twice -correct?_x000D_             Nick – I was incorrect in calling it the Dispatch on Demand fee (that is related to repairs only on our Internet fee schedule).  If a customer refuses a CSI we only charge the Technician Installation Fee: A one-time fee if you request / require a technician assisted installation. Additional fees or costs may apply if you request additional services during installation._x000D_ b. should it be immediate payment or deferred payment ( e.g., anniversary/monthly billing cycle )? – The business would like a configurable option to collect payment at point of sale or on billing date based on configurable business rules._x000D_ <Vas> Do we know or have the current business rules in the legacy that we can leverage on BSSe – _x000D_ Nick – Here is the support article on the process; OMS or OPUS would need to be asked on how they do it and with what rules._x000D_ 2. Should this dispatch on demand fee be in addition to one time tech install fee? – The business would like the Dispatch on Demand fee to be a configurable fee that can be added to customers that are eligible for CSI but refuse and request a FTI.  The dispatch on demand will be charged in place of the FTI fee.  There should not be a situation where both fees are required._x000D_ _x000D_ Product Response(BB 4):_x000D_ (LC) Allowing technicians to add service / equipment to orders is part of fulfillment process and should be integrated with RTI for shopping and order submission at or after the time of install.  Added to Fulfillment support for FTI epic.

~ The billing amount is withdrawn from payment type on the account. ~ An failed payment has a retry per the configurable values. ~ Payment is taken out the day the service is considered active upon billing completion ~ Bill cycle date is set to activation date. ~ A digital invoice is created for the payment. Supply Chain - Replacement order kits and enhancement to amend, change ordering for fulfillment.   Leilani Camacho - Create a replacement kit and the ability to reship equipment on the same order._x000D_  7/20 Questions from Architecture and corresponding responses from Product:_x000D_  "Reshipment functionality would give the ability to switch out equipment for lost in transit & swap equipment on the same order while its in flight_x000D_  1) New and existing cutomers in the middle of a swap_x000D_  2) Ability to access a pending order to make changes, updates or corrections for a varity of different reasons including but not limited to  - Reship equipment if the original equipment on the order was not received (lost in transit) - Customer requested changes to Service, Offer, or Address_x000D_  3) An order can only be revised up to the time PONR (Point of No Return) is reached_x000D_  4) Prior to PONR, when the order is revised for changes, systems should be notified of the pending revision_x000D_  5)No further changes are alllowed when PONR occurs_x000D_  6) PONR shoud occur after successful installation/activation when product inventory is updated and the billing begins_x000D_  7) Fallout systems should have the ability to access orders for correction outside the PONR limit for fallout correction handled by special agent role._x000D_

~OTS will use the Wireless Broadband service address  as the tax address when calculating taxes on the Wireless Broadband offer. ~ Wireless Broadband charge will be split into applicable components such as equipment, broadband, and HBO Max charges on the call to OTS with tax inclusive indicator set to Yes. ~ Wireless Broadband charge will be split into applicable components such as equipment, broadband, and HBO Max charges on the call to OTS with tax inclusive indicator set to No. ~ Wireless Broadband charge will be split into applicable components such as equipment, broadband, and HBO Max charges on the call to OTS with tax inclusive indicator set to No for HBO Max and Yes for equiptment and Yes for WBB.  ~AT&T Fees such as RCRF, ADMFEE, and PRPTAX  is deducted from one of the Wireless Broadband components and passed to OTS with tax inclusive indicator set to Yes. ~ For each component of the Wireless Broadband Offer, OTS returns the derived revenue amount and taxes. ~For the component which is to carry the flat fees, OTS returns the derived revenue amount, taxes (will include taxes on the charge and taxes on flat fees), and flat fees. ~AT&T Fees such as RCRF, ADMFEE, and PRPTAX are passed to OTS with tax inclusive indicator set to No. ~ OTS will return the sum of taxes, fees and revenue must equal the tax inclusive price(to ensure the customer is charged the advertised flat rate)

a. Need to ensure provisioning obtains the ICCID for the SIM, IMSI for ICCID, correct policy for the merged device and all other needed functionality to insure active data service. b. Provisioning should enforce a minimum required signal strength and signal quality (RSRP/RSRQ/SINR) (configurable), and block final activation until it is met.  c. Customer should get different login message based on a configurable threshold (5G or 4G) connection.  Threshold will help determine if customer has simple or standard setup. d. Customer should be kept in the ""Walled Garden"" until they finish Registration.  Based on information on sign up the actual registration the customer does when installing service is expected to be minimized. e. Response needs to be communicated to billing to start billing if customer meets minimum signal strength. **Speed Throuting limit (Configurable) **Capacity limits - to many customers on 1 tower  **Dynamic QoS network controls ~ Customer is able to use HSIA service from their home. ~ Customer should have Broadband IP address to insure they receive same service as a wired broadband customer. ~ Minimum required signal strength is reached in the customers location before service is considered active and notification is sent to billing. ~ Different login information provided to customer based on threshold value ~ Registration needs to be complete before customers is allowed beyond ""Walled Garden"".

Bus_Cap Component: Customer Contact if No Activation and manual order cancel
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: C1: I need to be able to cancel my WBB service, including wifi extenders if added to my order, after the device has shipped and prior to activation through digital or human assisted channels with an agent authorized to complete the cancellation and [optionally, via digital, configurable by region, state, fiber/copper eligibility, … ] C2: I need to provide a reason for my cancellation  C3: I need to understand the return process and easily return my device prior to cancelling my WBB service. C4: I need confirmation that my cancellation and return were successful   C5: I need an option to keep my WBB service but cancel my wifi extender service  C6: If I was required to make a pre-payment, I need to be refunded that amount  C7: If minimum service quality is not reached, the customer will be directed to contact care on the SHM app and the Splash screen. If the customer does not contact care in a configurable number of days the order is automatically canceled. C8: If customer does not activate service within a configurable number of days after delivery receipt, order is automatically canceled.

C8: If customer does not activate service within a configurable number of days after delivery receipt, order is automatically canceled.

I need the ability to view the customer’s CGW status (on line/ disconnected) and signal quality (high, medium low, poor ect..) through agent facing SHM ( Smart Home Mgr. Web dashboard) mirroring the customer’s view. I need the ability to view the number and location of Wi-Fi extenders connected on the customers home network (CGW and other devices). I need to see WBB network data and provided guided troubleshooting steps to resolve issues related to Network data/diagnostics such as: signal status and tower congestion related to the customer’s home network. Information should be presented in a simple manner  that is easily understood. When swapping equipment I need the ability to provide the customer with an expected date/time frame for the replacement. I need the ability to validate and update the customer’s shipping address when sending replacement (swap) equipment (CGW or Wi-Fi extenders). I need the ability to view the customer’s overall account status Such as: Suspend (Voluntary to Involuntary)/Cancelled/ Military leave/Past Due ect… I need the ability to handle lost or missing equipment charges on the customer's account.
Application Component: Customer Connect Salesforce Sales & Services Cloud

Human Assist Impact: Product owners should have the ability to define offers and promotions which are only available for existing WBB customers. Human Assist Impact: Agents can change/replace, add or remove Offers and promotions on existing WBB customers (The main offer can only be replaced). Human Assist Impact: Agent and customer must be able to define the Shipping address to utilize if equipment needs to be shipped       o Shipping address must be validated before the modify order can be submitted. Comms: Ability to send notifications regarding Modify service orders like New customer order notifications. Reporting: Ability for Product Owners to create reports as needed for items specific to Modify service orders. Human Assist Impact: Ability for authenticated and authorized agents to cancel a pending modify service order via human assisted channels        Ability to control which agents are able to cancel modify service orders based on current status of the order and agent job function. Any changes made due to the modify service order should be reverted and not updated on the account.  This includes the ability for equipment which is set to be removed due to the pending modify service order to remain on the account and active if cancelling the pending Modify service order.  If service was added, it should not be added when the modify service order is cancelled.  If service was removed, it should not be removed when the modify service order is cancelled.  This should be the same for all other changes which are supported on a Modify service order.

Ordering_x000D_   - Ability to order Extended Wifi Coverage Service (EWCS) via Online, Salesforce, Smart Home Manager (SHM), and any additional CARE support systems_x000D_   - Wireless Broadband (WBB) service and a residential gateway (RG) are required to order EWCS_x000D_         o Only the 6e Wifi Extender will be available with the CGW and CGW will only support the 6e Wifi Extender at this time_x000D_         oNote: In the future, 6e Wifi Extenders should support being moved to other services which also allow EWCS, if still utilizing the CGW, without having to return and reship equipment_x000D_  - Ability to define a configurable maximum number of extenders based on Sales Channel and customer type_x000D_         o Once EWCS is existing, additional extenders will only be available to add via specifically defined channels with a restriction to how many can be added at one time and up to the maximum allowed for that channel (Channels not defined to be able to add to existing service will be restricted from adding more extenders)_x000D_         o A customer or agent can determine there is a need to add additional equipment or accessories to improve service or meet an additional need up to the configured restrictions_x000D_         o If customer has more extenders than the current maximum, the customer can maintain the current assigned number of extenders._x000D_  - EWCS on WBB is Customer Self Install only_x000D_  - Ability to recommend the number of extenders needed for the customer (recommender tool)_x000D_         o Ability to allow the Recommender Tool by sales channel_x000D_         o Customer will be restricted to a maximum number of extenders based on the recommended number of extenders_x000D_  - Ability to bill Extended Wifi Coverage Service monthly _x000D_  - Ability to include EWCS within offers with other services or as its own charge_x000D_  - Ability to define promotions applicable to EWCS using existing promotion definition logic_x000D_  - Ability to charge a different MRC price based on customer type_x000D_  - Ability to collect prorated charges at the POS when EWCS or additional extenders are added mid-cycle. Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: As an AT&T business owner, I want the BSSe terms and conditions to be captured and available in the correct systems, including the audio recording required for all payment/ABP terms in the call center buy flow, so AT&T is saving and storing all required terms and conditions records appropriately.     Capability 1. Need a feed of the Bratislava reps' UUIDs into ECRMI (provided CinglePoint MOTS (CP) (att.com) has not already cared for this feed)  • Ongoing need  • Regularly update      Capability 2. Capture an audio recording of the agent reading the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms and the customer verbally agreeing. Those audio recordings must be stored for the applicable BAU broadband timeframes, mapped to the financial transactions related to the agreement, and searchable by business users who need to verify customers' acceptance of the terms.  Note: We are not impacting the normal legacy timeframes for all other products – Fiber, Mobility, etc.)  Capability 3. The existing CAPM mapping of payments to the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms audio recordings must differentiate BSSe payment transactions from Enabler, Telegence, and other billers.   Exclusive BSSe identifiers:  - RTB/RTB    - PRE/RTB     Capability 4. ECRMI processing needs to accommodate the BSSe Customer Graph Autopay Enrollment, Update, and De-enrollment transactions initiated by customers in any channel and map them to the correct short legal payment/ABP terms audio recordings when applicable.     Capability 5. One time batch “catch up” job to process all the BSSe call center agent initiated Autopay Enrollments, Updates, and De-enrollments since the launch of AIA. The batch job will capture the manually saved recordings and process them into the automated system that will apply the required transaction mapping and store the recordings for the applicable timeframe.  All manually saved recordings that are not applicable to any of the autopay changes will be purged. This catch up job must not purge or alter any other saved recordings in the repository.  Note: Before running this "catch up" job, any recordings or records that are part of any legal hold or have any other business reason requiring retention must be managed appropriately.     Capability 6. System Failure/Error Handling processing:  UC1: Verint audio recording fails  UC2: Transfer of Verint audio recording fails  • Added back to the work queue to retry  UC3: Agent mapping of audio recording fails  UC4: ECRMI output fails     Capability 7. The entire T&C key associated with the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms, must be stored in the customer agreement field for CAPM. CAPM requires that data to initiate data feeds into the appropriate ECDW tables. The full T&C key is provided in the CaaS/payment APIs and differentiates the sales channel, language, and specific version of the payment/ABP term accepted by the customer.     Capability 8: ALL BSSe AIA terms and conditions accepted by customers in any sales channel must be sent downstream into OPTICAL for storage and retention with the appropriate terms keys, customer identifiers, and date/time stamps. This includes the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms
Application Component: CCT ATT VERINT CONTACT QUALITY MONITORING

Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Online and MyAT&T app must support an Online promo/reward card/product offer flow to automatically add a reward card  promotion only.

As an AT&T business owner, I want the BSSe terms and conditions to be captured and available in the correct systems, including the audio recording required for all payment/ABP terms in the call center buy flow, so AT&T is saving and storing all required terms and conditions records appropriately.     Capability 1. Need a feed of the Bratislava reps' UUIDs into ECRMI (provided CinglePoint MOTS (CP) (att.com) has not already cared for this feed)  •    Ongoing need  •    Regularly update      Capability 2. Capture an audio recording of the agent reading the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms and the customer verbally agreeing. Those audio recordings must be stored for the applicable BAU broadband timeframes, mapped to the financial transactions related to the agreement, and searchable by business users who need to verify customers' acceptance of the terms.  Note: We are not impacting the normal legacy timeframes for all other products – Fiber, Mobility, etc.)  Capability 3. The existing CAPM mapping of payments to the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms audio recordings must differentiate BSSe payment transactions from Enabler, Telegence, and other billers.   Exclusive BSSe identifiers:  - RTB/RTB    - PRE/RTB     Capability 4. ECRMI processing needs to accommodate the BSSe Customer Graph Autopay Enrollment, Update, and De-enrollment transactions initiated by customers in any channel and map them to the correct short legal payment/ABP terms audio recordings when applicable.     Capability 5. One time batch “catch up” job to process all the BSSe call center agent initiated Autopay Enrollments, Updates, and De-enrollments since the launch of AIA. The batch job will capture the manually saved recordings and process them into the automated system that will apply the required transaction mapping and store the recordings for the applicable timeframe.  All manually saved recordings that are not applicable to any of the autopay changes will be purged. This catch up job must not purge or alter any other saved recordings in the repository. The entire T&C key associated with the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms, must be stored in the customer agreement field for CAPM. CAPM requires that data to initiate data feeds into the appropriate ECDW tables. The full T&C key is provided in the CaaS/payment APIs and differentiates the sales channel, language, and specific version of the payment/ABP term accepted by the customer.     Capability 8: ALL BSSe AIA terms and conditions accepted by customers in any sales channel must be sent downstream into OPTICAL for storage and retention with the appropriate terms keys, customer identifiers, and date/time stamps. This includes the short version of the legal payment/ABP terms

Bus_Cap Component: BSSe Device Financing Installment Loan Setup
Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Capability 1 – Determining Loan Eligibility  We need the ability to determine a customer is eligible for a connected device installment loan so that the business can make a reasonable determination as to whether they will be repaid.  Capability 2 – Loan Setup  Once a customer is determined eligible for device financing, we need the ability to create and setup the loan so that accounting and revenue can be tracked throughout the loan life cycle.  Capability 3 – Launch Control  Enable release on demand for the business, so that we can release the value to the customer as we decide based on our assessment of the market.  Capability 4 – Loan Cancellation and Fallout  We need the ability to cancel an installment loan when a customer changes their mind before the device ships, and we understand that issues can arise so we want to support fallout and error handling.

Capability 2 – Loan Setup  Once a customer is determined eligible for device financing, we need the ability to create and setup the loan so that accounting and revenue can be tracked throughout the loan life cycle.  Capability 3 – Launch Control  Enable release on demand for the business, so that we can release the value to the customer as we decide based on our assessment of the market.  Capability 4 – Loan Cancellation and Fallout  We need the ability to cancel an installment loan when a customer changes their mind before the device ships, and we understand that issues can arise so we want to support fallout and error handling.

Bus_Cap_Detail Qualifier: Issues from CLM perspective with existing solution for returns:  • Current comm is only set up for receipt at carrier. Customers can return directly to warehouse, so the content needs to be able to reflect that. Requires update to CRD  o Additionally, CLM needs to include this receipt at RL in reminder logic, as the customer has returned their equipment, so we should not remind them to return equipment  • There is a problem with RMA reconciliation not happening where a customer has returned their equipment prior to the RMA being created  o In this scenario, the RMA still will include the items that have already been returned, so the RMA created communication will be invalid, as it lists all items that need to be returned, including the item that has already been returned due to lack of reconciliation  o This scenario will continue to become problematic if CLM never receives the received at carrier or received at RL event, as CLM will never become aware of the equipment having already been returned, and therefore will send reminder comms to the customer to return the equipment (even though they already have!)  • Note: this does not impact NRF charge emails (from CLMs perspective) as that is a separate disconnected process. If the Return Past Due event is not published by OG, CLM will not send the NRF charge email
